   140	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: configuration shape for production ChainTape mode.
   141	///
   142	/// Production ChainTape runtime configuration.
   143	///
   144	/// Enabled by env var `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH=<runtime_repo_path>`.
   145	/// When unset, the evaluator falls back to legacy `TuringBus::new` /
   146	/// `TuringBus::with_wal_path` (no on-disk ChainTape).
   147	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   148	pub struct RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
   149	    /// Filesystem path to the on-disk runtime git repo.
   150	    /// `Git2LedgerWriter` rooted here writes `refs/transitions/main`.
   151	    /// `<runtime_repo_path>/rejections.jsonl` is the L4.E persistent file.
   152	    /// `<runtime_repo_path>/pinned_pubkeys.json` carries the per-run
   153	    /// `PinnedSystemPubkeys` so `verify_chaintape` (Atom 4) can re-verify
   154	    /// entry signatures without separate config.
   155	    pub runtime_repo_path: PathBuf,
   156	    /// CAS root directory. Distinct from `runtime_repo_path` so CAS payloads
   157	    /// can be inspected independently of the chain refs.
   158	    pub cas_path: PathBuf,
   159	    /// Run identity for evidence-dir naming + audit trail. Defaults to
   160	    /// `TURINGOS_RUN_ID` env var or current Unix-second timestamp.
   161	    pub run_id: String,
   162	    /// Sequencer mpsc channel capacity. Default 64.
   163	    pub queue_capacity: usize,
   164	    /// TB-G G1.1 (architect §8 SIGNED 2026-05-11; packet §2 + §5 Q4):
   165	    /// when `true` AND `refs/transitions/main` is non-empty,
   166	    /// `build_chaintape_sequencer` resumes the existing chain instead of
   167	    /// fail-closing with `BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo`. Default
   168	    /// `false` preserves back-compat for all pre-G-Phase callers (TB-N* /
   169	    /// Stage C / Wave 3 50p / TB-N3 Phase 2). Env-gated by
   170	    /// `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME == "1"` (strict equality; no
   171	    /// truthy-string footgun).
   172	    pub resume_existing_chain: bool,
   173	}
   174	
   175	impl RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
   176	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: env-flag-gated chaintape mode entry — evaluator calls this once at boot.
   177	    ///
   178	    /// Build from env. Returns `None` if `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH` unset.
   179	    pub fn from_env() -> Option<Self> {
   180	        let runtime_repo_path: PathBuf =
   181	            std::env::var("TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH").ok()?.into();
   182	        let cas_path: PathBuf = match std::env::var("TURINGOS_CAS_PATH") {
   183	            Ok(p) => p.into(),
   184	            Err(_) => runtime_repo_path
   185	                .parent()
   186	                .unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new("."))
   187	                .join(format!(
   188	                    "cas_{}",
   189	                    runtime_repo_path
   190	                        .file_name()
   191	                        .map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
   192	                        .unwrap_or_else(|| "default".into()),
   193	                )),
   194	        };
   195	        let run_id = std::env::var("TURINGOS_RUN_ID").unwrap_or_else(|_| {
   196	            std::time::SystemTime::now()
   197	                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   198	                .map(|d| d.as_secs().to_string())
   199	                .unwrap_or_else(|_| "0".into())
   200	        });
   201	        let queue_capacity = std::env::var("TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_QUEUE_CAPACITY")
   202	            .ok()
   203	            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<usize>().ok())
   204	            .unwrap_or(64);
   205	        // TB-G G1.1 (packet §5 Q4): strict equality on "1". `Ok("1")` flips
   206	        // the resume gate on; any other value (including "true", "yes",
   207	        // "TRUE", "01", " 1", absent) leaves the gate off. No
   208	        // truthy-string footgun.
   209	        let resume_existing_chain = matches!(
   210	            std::env::var("TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME").as_deref(),
   211	            Ok("1")
   212	        );
   213	        Some(Self {
   214	            runtime_repo_path,
   215	            cas_path,
   216	            run_id,
   217	            queue_capacity,
   218	            resume_existing_chain,
   219	        })
   220	    }
   221	}

   223	// ── Bundle returned by the factory ──────────────────────────────────────────
   224	
   225	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: handle bundle bridging evaluator → Sequencer + on-disk ChainTape.
   226	///
   227	/// Bundle of runtime handles produced by `build_chaintape_sequencer`.
   228	///
   229	/// The caller is responsible for wiring `sequencer` into a `TuringBus` via
   230	/// `TuringBus::with_sequencer(kernel, config, bundle.sequencer.clone())`,
   231	/// then driving the runtime to completion and calling `bundle.shutdown()`
   232	/// at exit to drain queued submissions.
   233	pub struct ChaintapeBundle {
   234	    /// Cloned and passed to `TuringBus::with_sequencer`.
   235	    pub sequencer: Arc<Sequencer>,
   236	    /// Concrete L4 writer (Git-backed). Test code holds a clone for chain-walk verification.
   237	    pub transition_writer: Arc<RwLock<dyn LedgerWriter>>,
   238	    /// L4.E rejection writer. JSONL backend (Atom 1.2 extension) when persisting; falls back
   239	    /// to in-memory if the JSONL path cannot be opened (caller error handling).
   240	    pub rejection_writer: Arc<RwLock<RejectionEvidenceWriter>>,
   241	    /// Per-run epoch — verifiers re-derive `pinned_pubkeys` map keyed by this epoch.
   242	    pub epoch: SystemEpoch,
   243	    /// Resolved runtime repo path (after canonicalization). Atom 5+ writes pinned pubkey
   244	    /// JSON + agent audit trail under this dir.
   245	    pub runtime_repo_path: PathBuf,
   246	    /// Resolved CAS root directory. Atom 5 callers re-open `CasStore` here to
   247	    /// write `AgentProposalRecord` artifacts (mirrors `runtime_repo_path` for
   248	    /// the L4 / L4.E side).
   249	    pub cas_path: PathBuf,
   250	    /// Driver task running `run_chaintape_driver` against the queue.
   251	    pub driver_handle: JoinHandle<()>,
   252	    /// Drain trigger. Caller invokes `bundle.shutdown().await` at evaluator exit.
   253	    pub shutdown_tx: oneshot::Sender<()>,
   254	}
   255	
   256	impl ChaintapeBundle {
   257	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: drain + clean-shutdown contract — caller invokes at evaluator exit.
   258	    ///
   259	    /// Drain + shutdown contract:
   260	    /// 1. Send shutdown signal (consumes shutdown_tx).
   261	    /// 2. Driver wrapper sees signal → closes queue_rx → drains remaining → exits.
   262	    /// 3. `driver_handle.await` blocks until drain completes.
   263	    /// 4. JoinError (panic) is wrapped into `DriverError::JoinError`; clean exit returns Ok.
   264	    pub async fn shutdown(self) -> Result<(), DriverError> {
   265	        let _ = self.shutdown_tx.send(());
   266	        match self.driver_handle.await {
   267	            Ok(()) => Ok(()),
   268	            Err(join_err) => Err(DriverError::JoinError(join_err.to_string())),
   269	        }
   270	    }
   271	}
   272	
   273	// ── Errors ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   274	
   275	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: bootstrap error class — fail-closed admission gate for production ChainTape.
   276	///
   277	/// Errors produced by the factory at bootstrap time.
   278	#[derive(Debug)]
   279	pub enum BootstrapError {
   280	    Io(std::io::Error),
   281	    LedgerWriter(String),
   282	    Cas(String),
   283	    Keypair(String),
   284	    /// Atom 1 fail-closed: refuse to bootstrap a `Sequencer` (which always
   285	    /// starts `next_logical_t = 0`) on top of an existing `refs/transitions/main`
   286	    /// chain — the next commit would mismatch `Git2LedgerWriter`'s strict
   287	    /// `len + 1` invariant. Resume mode is deferred to a future TB.
   288	    NonEmptyRuntimeRepo {
   289	        path: PathBuf,
   290	        existing_head: String,
   291	    },
   292	    /// TB-7 Atom 1.7 fail-closed (Codex audit cc7b3dd action item #1):
   293	    /// refuse to bootstrap when the L4.E rejection writer cannot open its
   294	    /// JSONL file. Silent fallback to in-memory L4.E is the same
   295	    /// anti-pattern as legacy `bus.append` as authoritative state mutation
   296	    /// — a chain-backed run that secretly drops L4.E writes is worse than
   297	    /// a failed boot.
   298	    RejectionWriter(String),
   299	}
   300	
   301	impl std::fmt::Display for BootstrapError {
   302	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   303	        match self {
   304	            Self::Io(e) => write!(f, "io error: {e}"),
   305	            Self::LedgerWriter(e) => write!(f, "ledger writer error: {e}"),
   306	            Self::Cas(e) => write!(f, "cas error: {e}"),
   307	            Self::Keypair(e) => write!(f, "keypair error: {e}"),
   308	            Self::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo {
   309	                path,
   310	                existing_head,
   311	            } => write!(
   312	                f,
   313	                "non-empty runtime repo at {path:?} (existing head {existing_head}); \
   314	                 TB-6 Atom 1 fail-closes here. Reconstruction from existing chain is \
   315	                 deferred to a future TB. Point TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH at a fresh \
   316	                 directory to start a new run."
   317	            ),
   318	            Self::RejectionWriter(e) => write!(f, "rejection writer error: {e}"),
   319	        }
   320	    }
   321	}
   322	
   323	impl std::error::Error for BootstrapError {}
   324	
   325	impl From<std::io::Error> for BootstrapError {
   326	    fn from(e: std::io::Error) -> Self {
   327	        Self::Io(e)
   328	    }
   329	}
   330

   332	///

   335	#[derive(Debug)]
   336	pub enum DriverError {

   339

   350	// ── Pinned pubkey on-disk format ────────────────────────────────────────────
   351	
   352	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: on-disk pinned-pubkey manifest — bridges Atom 1 keypair to Atom 4 verify_chaintape.
   353	///
   354	/// On-disk pinned-pubkey manifest. Written to `<runtime_repo>/pinned_pubkeys.json`
   355	/// at bootstrap so `verify_chaintape` (Atom 4) can re-verify `system_signature`
   356	/// on every `LedgerEntry` without separate config.
   357	#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
   358	pub struct PinnedPubkeyManifest {
   359	    pub run_id: String,
   360	    pub tb_id: String,
   361	    pub epoch: u64,
   362	    pub pubkeys: Vec<PinnedPubkeyEntry>,
   363	}
   364	
   365	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: single-epoch pinned pubkey row in the on-disk manifest.
   366	#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
   367	pub struct PinnedPubkeyEntry {
   368	    pub epoch: u64,
   369	    pub pubkey_hex: String,
   370	}
   371	
   372	const PINNED_PUBKEYS_FILENAME: &str = "pinned_pubkeys.json";
   373	const PINNED_PUBKEYS_TB_ID: &str = "TB-6";
   374	
   375	fn write_pinned_pubkey_manifest(
   376	    runtime_repo_path: &Path,
   377	    epoch: SystemEpoch,
   378	    keypair: &Ed25519Keypair,
   379	    run_id: &str,
   380	) -> Result<(), BootstrapError> {
   381	    let pubkey = keypair.public_key();
   382	    let pubkey_hex: String = pubkey
   383	        .as_bytes()
   384	        .iter()
   385	        .map(|b| format!("{:02x}", b))
   386	        .collect();
   387	    let manifest = PinnedPubkeyManifest {
   388	        run_id: run_id.to_string(),
   389	        tb_id: PINNED_PUBKEYS_TB_ID.to_string(),
   390	        epoch: epoch.get(),
   391	        pubkeys: vec![PinnedPubkeyEntry {
   392	            epoch: epoch.get(),
   393	            pubkey_hex,
   394	        }],
   395	    };
   396	    let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&manifest)
   397	        .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Io(std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)))?;
   398	    std::fs::create_dir_all(runtime_repo_path)?;
   399	    std::fs::write(runtime_repo_path.join(PINNED_PUBKEYS_FILENAME), json)?;
   400	    Ok(())
   401	}
   402	
   403	// ── Factory ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   404	
   405	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: production-mode factory — single entry-point that wires the kernel into an LLM-driven binary with on-disk chain persistence.
   406	///
   407	/// Build a production-mode `Sequencer` + `Git2LedgerWriter` + `RejectionEvidenceWriter` +
   408	/// driver task per `RuntimeChaintapeConfig`.
   409	///
   410	/// Steps:
   411	/// 1. Open `Git2LedgerWriter` at `config.runtime_repo_path`. **Fail-closed** if the
   412	///    repo already has a `refs/transitions/main` reference (non-empty chain) —
   413	///    resume mode is a future-TB enhancement.
   414	/// 2. Open `CasStore` at `config.cas_path`.
   415	/// 3. Generate a per-run `Ed25519Keypair`. Pin its public key to
   416	///    `PinnedSystemPubkeys` under `epoch`. Write `pinned_pubkeys.json`
   417	///    next to the runtime repo for `verify_chaintape`.
   418	/// 4. Build `RejectionEvidenceWriter` (Atom 1.1: in-memory; Atom 1.2 extends to
   419	///    JSONL-backed via `RejectionEvidenceWriter::open_jsonl`).
   420	/// 5. Initialize `QState::genesis()` (existing `QState::default()` constructor).
   421	/// 6. Construct `Sequencer::new(...)` — captures the queue receiver in the tuple return.
   422	/// 7. Spawn `run_chaintape_driver(sequencer.clone(), queue_rx, shutdown_rx)` on
   423	///    the current tokio runtime.
   424	/// 8. Return `ChaintapeBundle` with all handles.
   425	pub fn build_chaintape_sequencer(
   426	    config: &RuntimeChaintapeConfig,
   427	) -> Result<ChaintapeBundle, BootstrapError> {
   428	    build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q(config, QState::genesis())
   429	}
   430	
   431	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: TB-6 Atom 2 — factory variant accepting a pre-seeded `initial_q`.
   432	///
   433	/// Production-mode factory variant for callers that need to pre-populate the
   434	/// economic state (e.g., adapter-level tests + Atom 3 smoke fixtures that
   435	/// seed sponsor balance for `WorkTx` admission per WP § 18 Inv 5). The base
   436	/// `build_chaintape_sequencer` delegates here with `QState::genesis()`.
   437	///
   438	/// All other behavior (fail-closed on non-empty repo, pinned-pubkey manifest,
   439	/// JSONL-backed L4.E writer, runtime-side driver wrapper, Sequencer wiring) is
   440	/// identical to the base factory.
   441	pub fn build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q(
   442	    config: &RuntimeChaintapeConfig,
   443	    initial_q: QState,
   444	) -> Result<ChaintapeBundle, BootstrapError> {
   445	    // Step 1: open or init runtime repo. Branch on resume_existing_chain.
   446	    //
   447	    // TB-G G1.1 (architect §8 SIGNED 2026-05-11; packet §2): when
   448	    // `resume_existing_chain == true` AND `refs/transitions/main` is
   449	    // non-empty, take the resume path; otherwise preserve the original
   450	    // TB-6 fail-closed behavior (`NonEmptyRuntimeRepo`). Empty repo +
   451	    // resume=true degrades to fresh genesis (SG-G1.1 byte-equality).
   452	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&config.runtime_repo_path)?;
   453	    let git_writer = Git2LedgerWriter::open(&config.runtime_repo_path)
   454	        .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::LedgerWriter(e.to_string()))?;
   455	    let chain_is_non_empty = git_writer.head_commit_oid().is_some();
   456	    let resume_active = config.resume_existing_chain && chain_is_non_empty;
   457	
   458	    if chain_is_non_empty && !config.resume_existing_chain {
   459	        // SG-G1.4: existing TB-6 fail-closed gate preserved when resume
   460	        // is OFF. All TB-N* / Stage C / Wave 3 50p / TB-N3 Phase 2
   461	        // smoke runs hit this branch unchanged.
   462	        let existing_head = git_writer
   463	            .head_commit_oid()
   464	            .map(|o| o.to_string())
   465	            .unwrap_or_default();
   466	        return Err(BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo {
   467	            path: config.runtime_repo_path.clone(),
   468	            existing_head,
   469	        });
   470	    }
   471	
   472	    // Step 2: open CAS (same path for both branches; resume reads
   473	    // pre-existing payloads, fresh writes new ones).
   474	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&config.cas_path)?;
   475	    let cas_store = CasStore::open(&config.cas_path)
   476	        .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Cas(e.to_string()))?;
   477	
   478	    // Step 3-3.5: keypair + pinned pubkeys + seed QState + chain length.
   479	    //
   480	    // FRESH path: generate fresh keypair, write fresh manifest, seed
   481	    // Sequencer with caller-supplied `initial_q`, next_logical_t = 0.
   482	    //
   483	    // RESUME path (FC2 Boot constitutional alignment — "every real
   484	    // evidence run must be replayable from genesis_report + ChainTape
   485	    // + CAS + agent registry + system pubkeys", CLAUDE.md §3.2):
   486	    // read existing pinned_pubkeys.json (must exist; fail-closed if
   487	    // not) → build PinnedSystemPubkeys from all manifest entries →
   488	    // read existing initial_q_state.json (must exist; fail-closed
   489	    // if not) → replay every L4 entry via the canonical
   490	    // `replay_full_transition` primitive (the same primitive
   491	    // `verify_chaintape` uses; FC2-Boot replay determinism is
   492	    // covered by Stage A3 SG-A3.4 multi-ref replay-byte-equality)
   493	    // → generate a NEW keypair for a NEW epoch, append to manifest,
   494	    // pin the new pubkey. Old epochs remain pinned so prior entries
   495	    // still verify; new commits sign with the new epoch.
   496	    //
   497	    // Note re packet §2 adjacent-surfaces row: that row described
   498	    // `head_t_witness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` as the
   499	    // QState-rebuild primitive, but in code that helper only
   500	    // reconstructs the 6-field HeadTWitness derived view from L4 /
   501	    // L4.E / CAS ref OIDs (Stage A3 SG-A3.4 derived-view boundary).
   502	    // Per FC2 §3.2 + §4.1 G-009 Path C the canonical QState replay
   503	    // primitive is `replay_full_transition`, which is what
   504	    // `verify_chaintape` uses. This module therefore takes the
   505	    // canonical FC2 replay path; `reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs`
   506	    // is unused by G1.1.
   507	    let (keypair, epoch, pinned, seed_q, chain_length): (
   508	        Arc<Ed25519Keypair>,
   509	        SystemEpoch,
   510	        PinnedSystemPubkeys,
   511	        QState,
   512	        u64,
   513	    ) = if resume_active {
   514	        bootstrap_resume_state(&config.runtime_repo_path, &cas_store, &git_writer)?
   515	    } else {
   516	        let kp = Arc::new(
   517	            Ed25519Keypair::generate_with_secure_entropy()
   518	                .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Keypair(e.to_string()))?,
   519	        );
   520	        let epoch = SystemEpoch::new(1);
   521	        write_pinned_pubkey_manifest(&config.runtime_repo_path, epoch, &kp, &config.run_id)?;
   522	        let mut pinned = PinnedSystemPubkeys::new();
   523	        pinned.insert(epoch, kp.public_key());
   524	        (kp, epoch, pinned, initial_q, 0u64)
   525	    };
   526	    let pinned_pubkeys = Arc::new(pinned);
   527	
   528	    let transition_writer: Arc<RwLock<dyn LedgerWriter>> = Arc::new(RwLock::new(git_writer));
   529	    let cas = Arc::new(RwLock::new(cas_store));
   530	
   531	    // Step 4: rejection writer — JSONL-backed at <runtime_repo>/rejections.jsonl
   532	    // per Atom 1.2 + architect § 3.5 deliverable shape.
   533	    //
   534	    // **TB-7 Atom 1.7 (Codex audit cc7b3dd action item #1)**: fail-closed
   535	    // when JSONL open fails. Silent in-memory fallback is the same
   536	    // anti-pattern as legacy `bus.append` as authoritative state mutation:
   537	    // a chain-backed run that secretly drops L4.E writes is worse than a
   538	    // failed boot. ChainTape mode is contractually a fail-closed declaration.
   539	    //
   540	    // TB-G G1.1: on resume the existing rejections.jsonl is re-opened,
   541	    // preserving the cumulative L4.E head. `open_jsonl` internally
   542	    // verifies the chain and rejects any tamper.
   543	    let rejections_path = config.runtime_repo_path.join("rejections.jsonl");
   544	    let rejection_writer = match RejectionEvidenceWriter::open_jsonl(rejections_path.clone()) {
   545	        Ok(w) => Arc::new(RwLock::new(w)),
   546	        Err(e) => {
   547	            return Err(BootstrapError::RejectionWriter(format!(
   548	                "open_jsonl({:?}) failed: {e}",
   549	                rejections_path
   550	            )));
   551	        }
   552	    };
   553	
   554	    // Step 5: predicate + tool registries (default empty registries — production-binary
   555	    // is responsible for registering predicates / tools before submitting txs).
   556	    let predicate_registry = Arc::new(PredicateRegistry::new());
   557	    let tool_registry = Arc::new(ToolRegistry::new());
   558	
   559	    // Step 6: persist `initial_q_state.json` so `verify_chaintape` can
   560	    // replay from the same seed. TB-G G1.1: skip on resume — the file
   561	    // already exists from the original bootstrap; re-writing would
   562	    // overwrite the canonical genesis snapshot with the post-replay
   563	    // QState and break FC2 replay determinism.
   564	    if !resume_active {
   565	        let initial_q_path = config.runtime_repo_path.join("initial_q_state.json");
   566	        let initial_q_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&seed_q)
   567	            .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Cas(format!("initial_q serialize: {e}")))?;
   568	        std::fs::write(&initial_q_path, initial_q_json)?;
   569	    }
   570	
   571	    // Step 7: construct Sequencer. Fresh path uses `Sequencer::new`
   572	    // (next_logical_t = 0); resume path uses
   573	    // `Sequencer::new_at_logical_t(.., chain_length)` so the next
   574	    // commit satisfies `Git2LedgerWriter::append`'s strict
   575	    // `len + 1` invariant (packet §3 SG-G1.2).
   576	    let (sequencer, queue_rx) = if resume_active {
   577	        Sequencer::new_at_logical_t(
   578	            cas,
   579	            keypair,
   580	            epoch,
   581	            transition_writer.clone(),
   582	            rejection_writer.clone(),
   583	            predicate_registry,
   584	            tool_registry,
   585	            pinned_pubkeys,
   586	            seed_q,
   587	            config.queue_capacity,
   588	            chain_length,
   589	        )
   590	    } else {
   591	        Sequencer::new(
   592	            cas,
   593	            keypair,
   594	            epoch,
   595	            transition_writer.clone(),
   596	            rejection_writer.clone(),
   597	            predicate_registry,
   598	            tool_registry,
   599	            pinned_pubkeys,
   600	            seed_q,
   601	            config.queue_capacity,
   602	        )
   603	    };
   604	    let sequencer = Arc::new(sequencer);
   605	
   606	    // Step 8: spawn driver wrapper + shutdown channel.
   607	    let (shutdown_tx, shutdown_rx) = oneshot::channel::<()>();
   608	    let driver_seq = sequencer.clone();
   609	    let driver_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
   610	        run_chaintape_driver(driver_seq, queue_rx, shutdown_rx).await;
   611	    });
   612	
   613	    Ok(ChaintapeBundle {
   614	        sequencer,
   615	        transition_writer,
   616	        rejection_writer,
   617	        epoch,
   618	        runtime_repo_path: config.runtime_repo_path.clone(),
   619	        cas_path: config.cas_path.clone(),
   620	        driver_handle,
   621	        shutdown_tx,
   622	    })
   623	}
   624	
   625	/// TB-G G1.1 (architect §8 SIGNED 2026-05-11; packet §2 resume branch):
   626	/// reconstruct the (keypair, epoch, pinned pubkeys, seed QState,
   627	/// chain_length) tuple consumed by `build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q`
   628	/// when resuming a non-empty `refs/transitions/main` chain.
   629	///
   630	/// Steps (FC2 Boot constitutional alignment per CLAUDE.md §3.2 +
   631	/// §4.1 G-009 Path C):
   632	/// 1. Read existing `pinned_pubkeys.json`. Fail-closed if missing or
   633	///    unparseable.
   634	/// 2. Build `PinnedSystemPubkeys` from every manifest entry so all
   635	///    prior-epoch entries still verify.
   636	/// 3. Read existing `initial_q_state.json`. Fail-closed if missing.
   637	/// 4. Read every L4 entry from `refs/transitions/main`.
   638	/// 5. Replay via `replay_full_transition` — the canonical FC2 Boot
   639	///    replay primitive shared with `verify_chaintape`. Any replay
   640	///    failure is fail-closed (no partial admission per packet §5 Q1).
   641	/// 6. Generate a NEW keypair for a NEW epoch (`max_existing + 1`).
   642	///    Append the new entry to `pinned_pubkeys.json` on disk; old
   643	///    entries stay so prior commits still verify. Pin the new pubkey
   644	///    so new commits round-trip.
   645	fn bootstrap_resume_state(
   646	    runtime_repo_path: &Path,
   647	    cas_store: &CasStore,
   648	    git_writer: &Git2LedgerWriter,
   649	) -> Result<
   650	    (
   651	        Arc<Ed25519Keypair>,
   652	        SystemEpoch,
   653	        PinnedSystemPubkeys,
   654	        QState,
   655	        u64,
   656	    ),
   657	    BootstrapError,
   658	> {
   659	    // 1. Pinned pubkeys manifest — fail-closed if missing.
   660	    let manifest_path = runtime_repo_path.join(PINNED_PUBKEYS_FILENAME);
   661	    if !manifest_path.exists() {
   662	        return Err(BootstrapError::Keypair(format!(
   663	            "resume mode: pinned_pubkeys.json missing at {manifest_path:?}; \
   664	             cannot rebuild PinnedSystemPubkeys map for chain signature verification"
   665	        )));
   666	    }
   667	    let manifest_json = std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path)?;
   668	    let mut manifest: PinnedPubkeyManifest = serde_json::from_str(&manifest_json)
   669	        .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Keypair(format!("pinned_pubkeys.json parse: {e}")))?;
   670	
   671	    // 2. Rehydrate PinnedSystemPubkeys from every manifest entry.
   672	    let mut pinned = PinnedSystemPubkeys::new();
   673	    let mut max_epoch: u64 = 0;
   674	    for entry in &manifest.pubkeys {
   675	        let bytes = decode_pubkey_hex_32(&entry.pubkey_hex)?;
   676	        pinned.insert(SystemEpoch::new(entry.epoch), SystemPublicKey::from_bytes(bytes));
   677	        if entry.epoch > max_epoch {
   678	            max_epoch = entry.epoch;
   679	        }
   680	    }
   681	
   682	    // 3. Initial QState — fail-closed if missing.
   683	    let initial_q_path = runtime_repo_path.join("initial_q_state.json");
   684	    if !initial_q_path.exists() {
   685	        return Err(BootstrapError::Cas(format!(
   686	            "resume mode: initial_q_state.json missing at {initial_q_path:?}; \
   687	             cannot replay chain without canonical genesis seed QState"
   688	        )));
   689	    }
   690	    let initial_q_json = std::fs::read_to_string(&initial_q_path)?;
   691	    let initial_q: QState = serde_json::from_str(&initial_q_json)
   692	        .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Cas(format!("initial_q_state.json parse: {e}")))?;
   693	
   694	    // 4. Read every L4 entry.
   695	    let chain_length = git_writer.len();
   696	    let entries: Vec<LedgerEntry> = (1..=chain_length)
   697	        .map(|t| git_writer.read_at(t))
   698	        .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()
   699	        .map_err(|e| {
   700	            BootstrapError::LedgerWriter(format!("resume read_at sweep: {e}"))
   701	        })?;
   702	
   703	    // 5. Replay via the canonical FC2 Boot primitive.
   704	    //
   705	    // Production binaries register predicates / tools AFTER bootstrap,
   706	    // and `verify_chaintape` does the same — so empty registries here
   707	    // mirror `verify_chaintape`'s replay envelope. Replay is
   708	    // deterministic regardless of registry contents because
   709	    // `dispatch_transition` is a pure function over inputs.
   710	    let predicate_registry = PredicateRegistry::new();
   711	    let tool_registry = ToolRegistry::new();
   712	    let replayed_q = replay_full_transition(
   713	        &initial_q,
   714	        &entries,
   715	        cas_store,
   716	        &pinned,
   717	        &predicate_registry,
   718	        &tool_registry,
   719	    )
   720	    .map_err(|e| {

exec

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! TB-G G1.1 (architect §8 SIGNED 2026-05-11 "好，确认可以 ship";
     2	//! Canonical multi-clause Class-4 §8) — resume-mode genesis branch ship
     3	//! gates SG-G1.1..SG-G1.5.
     4	//!
     5	//! Architect verdict §1.3 verbatim (G-Phase directive):
     6	//!
     7	//! > 每个 problem fresh runtime_repo + fresh genesis — 每轮开局都把交易
     8	//! > 员洗白、清仓、重置记忆。市场不会涌现。
     9	//!
    10	//! G1.1 closes the loop by adding an env-gated resume admission path:
    11	//! `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME=1` lets `build_chaintape_sequencer` open a
    12	//! non-empty `refs/transitions/main` instead of fail-closing with
    13	//! `BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo`. Default-deny posture is
    14	//! preserved per CLAUDE.md §11 (resume=0 still fail-closes on non-empty
    15	//! repos — SG-G1.4 back-compat regression gate).
    16	//!
    17	//! FC-trace: FC2-Boot — every real evidence run must be replayable from
    18	//! `genesis_report + ChainTape + CAS + agent registry + system pubkeys`
    19	//! (CLAUDE.md §3.2). Resume IS the boot-time instance of that replay,
    20	//! seeded by `<runtime_repo>/initial_q_state.json` and consumed by the
    21	//! canonical `replay_full_transition` primitive shared with
    22	//! `verify_chaintape`. Existing Stage A3 SG-A3.4 covers replay
    23	//! byte-equality; G1.1 layers SG-G1.3 balances-byte-equal + SG-G1.5
    24	//! pinned-pubkey continuity on top.
    25	
    26	use tempfile::TempDir;
    27	use turingosv4::bus::{BusConfig, TuringBus};
    28	use turingosv4::economy::money::MicroCoin;
    29	use turingosv4::kernel::Kernel;
    30	use turingosv4::runtime::adapter::{genesis_with_balances, make_synthetic_task_open};
    31	use turingosv4::runtime::{
    32	    build_chaintape_sequencer, build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q, BootstrapError,
    33	    RuntimeChaintapeConfig,
    34	};
    35	use turingosv4::state::q_state::{AgentId, Hash};
    36	
    37	fn cfg_resume(tmp: &TempDir, run_id: &str, resume: bool) -> RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
    38	    RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
    39	        runtime_repo_path: tmp.path().join("runtime_repo"),
    40	        cas_path: tmp.path().join("cas"),
    41	        run_id: run_id.to_string(),
    42	        queue_capacity: 16,
    43	        resume_existing_chain: resume,
    44	    }
    45	}
    46	
    47	// ── SG-G1.1 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    48	//
    49	// Resume on empty repo == legacy genesis. With `resume_existing_chain=true`
    50	// but no existing chain, the resume short-circuit (`resume_active = false`
    51	// when `head_commit_oid().is_none()`) falls through to the fresh-genesis
    52	// path. Result: byte-equal `QState` to the legacy
    53	// `build_chaintape_sequencer` call.
    54	#[tokio::test]
    55	async fn sg_g1_1_resume_on_empty_repo_equals_legacy_genesis() {
    56	    // Fresh-genesis path with resume=true on an empty repo.
    57	    let tmp_a = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir_a");
    58	    let cfg_a = cfg_resume(&tmp_a, "g1_1-a", true);
    59	    let bundle_a = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_a)
    60	        .expect("resume=true on empty repo bootstraps fresh (G1.1 SG-G1.1)");
    61	    let q_a = bundle_a.sequencer.q_snapshot().expect("q_snapshot a");
    62	    bundle_a.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown a");
    63	
    64	    // Same fresh-genesis path with resume=false.
    65	    let tmp_b = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir_b");
    66	    let cfg_b = cfg_resume(&tmp_b, "g1_1-b", false);
    67	    let bundle_b = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_b).expect("legacy bootstrap b");
    68	    let q_b = bundle_b.sequencer.q_snapshot().expect("q_snapshot b");
    69	    bundle_b.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown b");
    70	
    71	    // Both QStates derive from `QState::genesis()` (same seed, no
    72	    // submitted txs, no economic mutation). Compare canonical roots —
    73	    // bit-exact equality across both branches.
    74	    assert_eq!(
    75	        q_a.state_root_t, q_b.state_root_t,
    76	        "SG-G1.1: state_root_t must match between resume=true/empty and resume=false/empty"
    77	    );
    78	    assert_eq!(
    79	        q_a.ledger_root_t, q_b.ledger_root_t,
    80	        "SG-G1.1: ledger_root_t must match"
    81	    );
    82	    assert_eq!(
    83	        q_a.economic_state_t, q_b.economic_state_t,
    84	        "SG-G1.1: economic_state_t must be byte-equal across branches"
    85	    );
    86	}
    87	
    88	// ── SG-G1.2 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    89	//
    90	// Resume on an N-entry chain sets `Sequencer.next_logical_t == N`. The
    91	// next commit's `Git2LedgerWriter::append` strict `len + 1` invariant
    92	// holds — proved by submitting one extra TaskOpen after resume and
    93	// observing the chain length advances from N → N+1.
    94	//
    95	// The test uses N=1 because making each subsequent `make_synthetic_task_open`
    96	// accept requires threading `parent_state_root` through the latest
    97	// `q_snapshot()` between submits, which races the async driver. N=1
    98	// fully proves the SG-G1.2 constitutional invariant
    99	// (`next_logical_t == chain_length`); the post-resume commit advances
   100	// 1 → 2 and pins the `Git2LedgerWriter` `len + 1` constraint.
   101	#[tokio::test]
   102	async fn sg_g1_2_resume_on_n_entry_chain_sets_next_logical_t_to_n() {
   103	    use turingosv4::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::LedgerWriter;
   104	
   105	    let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   106	    let cfg_fresh = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_2-fresh", false);
   107	
   108	    // Phase 1: fresh bootstrap, submit 1 TaskOpen (parent matches
   109	    // QState::genesis state_root = Hash::ZERO so it accepts).
   110	    let bundle = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_fresh).expect("fresh bootstrap");
   111	    let kernel = Kernel::new();
   112	    let bus = TuringBus::with_sequencer(kernel, BusConfig::default(), bundle.sequencer.clone());
   113	    let tx = make_synthetic_task_open("task-g1_2", "sponsor-g1_2", Hash::ZERO, "g1_2-1");
   114	    bus.submit_typed_tx(tx).await.expect("submit TaskOpen");
   115	    bundle.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown phase 1");
   116	    drop(bus);
   117	
   118	    // Reopen the writer to confirm chain length on disk.
   119	    let n_on_disk = {
   120	        let reopened = turingosv4::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::Git2LedgerWriter::open(
   121	            &cfg_fresh.runtime_repo_path,
   122	        )
   123	        .expect("reopen writer");
   124	        reopened.len()
   125	    };
   126	    assert_eq!(
   127	        n_on_disk, 1,
   128	        "phase 1: chain should hold 1 accepted L4 entry before resume"
   129	    );
   130	
   131	    // Phase 2: resume bootstrap. next_logical_t must equal 1.
   132	    let cfg_r = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_2-resume", true);
   133	    let bundle_r = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_r).expect("resume bootstrap");
   134	    assert_eq!(
   135	        bundle_r.sequencer.next_logical_t_peek(),
   136	        1,
   137	        "SG-G1.2: Sequencer.next_logical_t must equal chain_length on resume \
   138	         (chain has 1 entry → next_logical_t must be 1; the next commit signs as logical_t=2)"
   139	    );
   140	
   141	    // Phase 3: submit one more TaskOpen with parent = post-replay
   142	    // state_root. Chain advances 1 → 2 without
   143	    // `Git2LedgerWriter::append`'s strict `len + 1` invariant tripping.
   144	    let q_after_replay = bundle_r
   145	        .sequencer
   146	        .q_snapshot()
   147	        .expect("q_snapshot post-resume");
   148	    let kernel2 = Kernel::new();
   149	    let bus2 =
   150	        TuringBus::with_sequencer(kernel2, BusConfig::default(), bundle_r.sequencer.clone());
   151	    let tx_extra = make_synthetic_task_open(
   152	        "task-g1_2-post-resume",
   153	        "sponsor-g1_2",
   154	        q_after_replay.state_root_t,
   155	        "g1_2-post",
   156	    );
   157	    bus2.submit_typed_tx(tx_extra)
   158	        .await
   159	        .expect("submit post-resume TaskOpen");
   160	    bundle_r.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown phase 3");
   161	    drop(bus2);
   162	
   163	    let n_after = {
   164	        let reopened = turingosv4::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::Git2LedgerWriter::open(
   165	            &cfg_r.runtime_repo_path,
   166	        )
   167	        .expect("reopen writer");
   168	        reopened.len()
   169	    };
   170	    assert_eq!(
   171	        n_after, 2,
   172	        "SG-G1.2: chain length must advance from 1 → 2 after one post-resume commit"
   173	    );
   174	}
   175	
   176	// ── SG-G1.3 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   177	//
   178	// Balances reconstruction matches forward replay. A pre-seeded
   179	// `genesis_with_balances` QState carried through a forward run produces
   180	// `balances_t_A`; the same chain replayed via resume produces
   181	// `balances_t_B`. The two must be byte-equal — that's the
   182	// constitutional FC2 replay-determinism guarantee + Stage A3 SG-A3.4
   183	// generalized down to per-account balances.
   184	#[tokio::test]
   185	async fn sg_g1_3_resume_balances_reconstruction_matches_forward_replay() {
   186	    let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   187	    let cfg_fresh = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_3-fresh", false);
   188	
   189	    let alice = AgentId("alice-g1_3".into());
   190	    let bob = AgentId("bob-g1_3".into());
   191	    let initial_q = genesis_with_balances(&[
   192	        (alice.clone(), MicroCoin::from_coin(7).unwrap()),
   193	        (bob.clone(), MicroCoin::from_coin(11).unwrap()),
   194	    ]);
   195	
   196	    // Phase 1: forward run with pre-seeded balances + one TaskOpen.
   197	    // Clone the sequencer Arc *before* shutdown so q_snapshot post-drain
   198	    // observes the final applied state (driver may still be processing
   199	    // mid-submit; only post-shutdown is the canonical observation point).
   200	    let bundle =
   201	        build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q(&cfg_fresh, initial_q.clone()).expect("fresh");
   202	    let seq_forward = bundle.sequencer.clone();
   203	    let kernel = Kernel::new();
   204	    let bus = TuringBus::with_sequencer(kernel, BusConfig::default(), bundle.sequencer.clone());
   205	    let tx = make_synthetic_task_open("task-g1_3-1", "sponsor-g1_3", Hash::ZERO, "g1_3-1");
   206	    bus.submit_typed_tx(tx).await.expect("submit TaskOpen");
   207	    bundle.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown forward");
   208	    drop(bus);
   209	    let q_forward = seq_forward.q_snapshot().expect("q_snapshot forward");
   210	
   211	    // Phase 2: resume — replay reconstructs balances from initial_q +
   212	    // chain entries. Must produce byte-equal balances_t to forward run.
   213	    let cfg_r = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_3-resume", true);
   214	    let bundle_r = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_r).expect("resume bootstrap");
   215	    let q_resumed = bundle_r.sequencer.q_snapshot().expect("q_snapshot resumed");
   216	    bundle_r.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown resume");
   217	
   218	    // Per-account assertion + full-map assertion. The per-account
   219	    // assertion gives a more readable failure mode if the test ever
   220	    // breaks; the full-map assertion catches any extra account that
   221	    // shouldn't exist (or a missing one).
   222	    assert_eq!(
   223	        q_resumed
   224	            .economic_state_t
   225	            .balances_t
   226	            .0
   227	            .get(&alice)
   228	            .copied()
   229	            .unwrap_or_else(MicroCoin::zero),
   230	        q_forward
   231	            .economic_state_t
   232	            .balances_t
   233	            .0
   234	            .get(&alice)
   235	            .copied()
   236	            .unwrap_or_else(MicroCoin::zero),
   237	        "SG-G1.3: alice balance must match between forward and resumed run"
   238	    );
   239	    assert_eq!(
   240	        q_resumed
   241	            .economic_state_t
   242	            .balances_t
   243	            .0
   244	            .get(&bob)
   245	            .copied()
   246	            .unwrap_or_else(MicroCoin::zero),
   247	        q_forward
   248	            .economic_state_t
   249	            .balances_t
   250	            .0
   251	            .get(&bob)
   252	            .copied()
   253	            .unwrap_or_else(MicroCoin::zero),
   254	        "SG-G1.3: bob balance must match"
   255	    );
   256	    assert_eq!(
   257	        q_resumed.economic_state_t.balances_t, q_forward.economic_state_t.balances_t,
   258	        "SG-G1.3: full balances_t map must be byte-equal across forward / resumed runs"
   259	    );
   260	    // Bound the state root for free — if balances diverge, state_root
   261	    // diverges; this is the constitutional FC2 replay-determinism
   262	    // guarantee applied to the entire economic_state.
   263	    assert_eq!(
   264	        q_resumed.state_root_t, q_forward.state_root_t,
   265	        "SG-G1.3: state_root_t must be byte-equal between forward and resumed run"
   266	    );
   267	}
   268	
   269	// ── SG-G1.4 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   270	//
   271	// Back-compat regression gate. With `resume_existing_chain=false`, a
   272	// non-empty `refs/transitions/main` still produces the original TB-6
   273	// `BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo`. All TB-N* / Stage C / Wave 3
   274	// 50p / TB-N3 Phase 2 smoke runs hit this branch unchanged.
   275	#[tokio::test]
   276	async fn sg_g1_4_non_empty_runtime_repo_only_fires_when_resume_false() {
   277	    let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   278	    let cfg = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_4-fresh", false);
   279	
   280	    // Phase 1: fresh bootstrap, submit one TaskOpen to make the chain non-empty.
   281	    let bundle = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg).expect("fresh bootstrap");
   282	    let kernel = Kernel::new();
   283	    let bus = TuringBus::with_sequencer(kernel, BusConfig::default(), bundle.sequencer.clone());
   284	    let tx = make_synthetic_task_open("task-g1_4", "sponsor-g1_4", Hash::ZERO, "g1_4");
   285	    bus.submit_typed_tx(tx).await.expect("submit TaskOpen");
   286	    bundle.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown");
   287	    drop(bus);
   288	
   289	    // Phase 2: re-bootstrap with resume=false on the non-empty repo.
   290	    // Must fail-closed with NonEmptyRuntimeRepo (the original TB-6 gate).
   291	    let cfg_reboot = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_4-reboot", false);
   292	    let result = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_reboot);
   293	    match result {
   294	        Err(BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo {
   295	            path,
   296	            existing_head,
   297	        }) => {
   298	            assert_eq!(
   299	                path, cfg_reboot.runtime_repo_path,
   300	                "SG-G1.4: NonEmptyRuntimeRepo must echo the rejected runtime_repo_path"
   301	            );
   302	            assert!(
   303	                !existing_head.is_empty(),
   304	                "SG-G1.4: existing_head must be set when fail-closing"
   305	            );
   306	        }
   307	        Err(other) => panic!(
   308	            "SG-G1.4: expected BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo, got {other:?}"
   309	        ),
   310	        Ok(_) => panic!(
   311	            "SG-G1.4: resume=false on non-empty repo must NOT bootstrap successfully \
   312	             (would mask the TB-6 fail-closed gate for all prior smoke runs)"
   313	        ),
   314	    }
   315	
   316	    // Phase 3: same non-empty repo bootstraps cleanly when resume=true.
   317	    // Pins that the gap between "fail-closed" and "succeed" is exactly
   318	    // the `resume_existing_chain` field.
   319	    let cfg_resume = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_4-resume", true);
   320	    let bundle_r =
   321	        build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_resume).expect("resume=true on non-empty repo");
   322	    bundle_r.shutdown().await.expect("resume shutdown");
   323	}
   324	
   325	// ── SG-G1.5 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   326	//
   327	// Pinned-pubkey continuity. After resume, the original epoch's pubkey
   328	// entry MUST still be present in `pinned_pubkeys.json` (so prior L4
   329	// entries continue to verify). The manifest gains a NEW entry for the
   330	// new epoch — because Ed25519 secret keys are not persisted to disk,
   331	// resume cannot reuse the prior signing key; instead it generates a
   332	// new keypair for a new epoch + appends to the manifest. This is the
   333	// only correct way to preserve verification continuity for older
   334	// entries while letting the resumed sequencer sign new ones.
   335	#[tokio::test]
   336	async fn sg_g1_5_pinned_pubkeys_preserved_across_resume() {
   337	    let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   338	    let cfg = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_5-fresh", false);
   339	
   340	    // Phase 1: fresh bootstrap writes the initial manifest.
   341	    let bundle = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg).expect("fresh bootstrap");
   342	    let manifest_path = cfg.runtime_repo_path.join("pinned_pubkeys.json");
   343	    let original_json = std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path).expect("read original manifest");
   344	    let original: serde_json::Value =
   345	        serde_json::from_str(&original_json).expect("parse original manifest");
   346	    let original_epoch = original["epoch"].as_u64().expect("epoch u64");
   347	    assert_eq!(original_epoch, 1, "fresh manifest pins epoch=1");
   348	    let original_pubkey_hex = original["pubkeys"][0]["pubkey_hex"]
   349	        .as_str()
   350	        .expect("pubkey_hex")
   351	        .to_string();
   352	
   353	    // Submit one TaskOpen so the chain is non-empty (resume admission requires it).
   354	    let kernel = Kernel::new();
   355	    let bus = TuringBus::with_sequencer(kernel, BusConfig::default(), bundle.sequencer.clone());
   356	    let tx = make_synthetic_task_open("task-g1_5", "sponsor-g1_5", Hash::ZERO, "g1_5");
   357	    bus.submit_typed_tx(tx).await.expect("submit TaskOpen");
   358	    bundle.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown phase 1");
   359	    drop(bus);
   360	
   361	    // Phase 2: resume — manifest must still contain the original entry
   362	    // (epoch=1 pubkey unchanged) plus one new entry (epoch=2 with a
   363	    // different pubkey).
   364	    let cfg_r = cfg_resume(&tmp, "g1_5-resume", true);
   365	    let bundle_r = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg_r).expect("resume bootstrap");
   366	    let resumed_json =
   367	        std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path).expect("read manifest after resume");
   368	    let resumed: serde_json::Value =
   369	        serde_json::from_str(&resumed_json).expect("parse manifest after resume");
   370	    let resumed_pubkeys = resumed["pubkeys"]
   371	        .as_array()
   372	        .expect("pubkeys after resume must be an array");
   373	
   374	    assert!(
   375	        resumed_pubkeys.len() >= 2,
   376	        "SG-G1.5: manifest must gain at least one entry on resume (had {} after resume)",
   377	        resumed_pubkeys.len()
   378	    );
   379	    let original_still_present = resumed_pubkeys.iter().any(|e| {
   380	        e["epoch"].as_u64() == Some(original_epoch)
   381	            && e["pubkey_hex"].as_str() == Some(original_pubkey_hex.as_str())
   382	    });
   383	    assert!(
   384	        original_still_present,
   385	        "SG-G1.5: original (epoch={original_epoch}, pubkey={original_pubkey_hex}) MUST still be \
   386	         present in pinned_pubkeys.json after resume — prior L4 entries depend on this \
   387	         pubkey for system_signature verification"
   388	    );
   389	
   390	    let new_epoch = resumed["epoch"].as_u64().expect("epoch u64 after resume");
   391	    assert!(
   392	        new_epoch > original_epoch,
   393	        "SG-G1.5: top-level manifest epoch must advance on resume (was {original_epoch}, \
   394	         after resume {new_epoch})"
   395	    );
   396	    let new_entry_exists = resumed_pubkeys.iter().any(|e| {
   397	        e["epoch"].as_u64() == Some(new_epoch)
   398	            && e["pubkey_hex"].as_str().is_some()
   399	            && e["pubkey_hex"].as_str() != Some(original_pubkey_hex.as_str())
   400	    });
   401	    assert!(
   402	        new_entry_exists,
   403	        "SG-G1.5: a NEW (epoch={new_epoch}) entry with a distinct pubkey must be appended \
   404	         on resume — Ed25519 secrets aren't persisted, so the new sequencer must sign \
   405	         new L4 entries with a freshly generated keypair"
   406	    );
   407	
   408	    bundle_r.shutdown().await.expect("resume shutdown");
   409	}

164-"experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs" = "36b550c983623bfc326560f6f705be13e8d675859aeea1c93fbe3988a075731c"  # rehashed by TB-N3 A3 + A4 hookup (2026-05-11; architect ruling 2026-05-11 amendments 3-6 + Q1+Q2+Q6 + Q7 + amendment 5): (1) full-proof OMEGA WorkTx accept site (post tb8_await_state_root_advance ~line 2772) + per-tactic OMEGA WorkTx accept site (~line 3661) BOTH gain a `TURINGOS_TB_N3_AUTO_MARKET=1`-gated call to `tb_n3_emit_node_market_after_work_accept(&bundle.sequencer, &work_tx_id, &mut reg, "a3-omega-{work_tx_id}", logical_t, seed_micro)` — outcome typed-matched (Created/AlreadyExists/BudgetExhausted/SubmitFailed/PollTimeout) and logged best-effort via info!/warn!. seed_micro reads `TURINGOS_DEFAULT_POOL_SEED_MICRO` env (default 100_000 per architect Q1). (2) Same-task market context A4 wire-up at the build_agent_prompt site (~line 2114): when TB-N3 enabled, derives same-task accepted WorkTx list by walking q.economic_state_t.cpmm_pools_t for event_ids matching `node_survive:` prefix containing the current `task-{run_id}` substring (same-task isolation per amendment 5), then calls `turingosv4::sdk::market_context::render_market_context(&q, &task_id, &same_task_work_tx_ids, K, &agent_id)` with K from `TURINGOS_TB_N3_MARKET_CONTEXT_K` env (default 10 per architect Q7). Result appended to existing TB-14 PriceIndex `market_ticker_str`. Predecessor TB-N3 A2 hash 9942fdf1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N3 A2 (2026-05-11; architect ruling 2026-05-11 amendments 1+2 + Q5; charter v3 §3): replaced TB-9-era `invest_disabled_tb9` no-op handler at line 3008 with full TB-N3 A2 invest-routing pipeline. Reads `action.node` (canonical work_tx_id), `action.amount` (microCoin via f64 → i64 cast), `action.direction` ("long"|"yes" → BuyYes; "short"|"no" → BuyNo). When chaintape + agent_keypairs both present: q_snapshot → tb_n3_invest_to_router_tx pre-classify (snapshot mode) → on Ok submit + tb8_await_state_root_advance + write `MarketDecisionTrace::submitted` to CAS via `write_market_decision_trace_to_cas`; on Err map to `NoTradeReason` + write `MarketDecisionTrace::no_trade` to CAS. tool_dist counters: `invest_attempted`, `invest_submitted`, `invest_no_trade_<reason>`, `invest_submit_err`, `invest_snapshot_err`, `invest_no_chaintape`. event_id constructed via `node_survive_event_id` (architect amendment 1: "node market 的 event_id 必须是 accepted WorkTx 的 canonical tx_id，而不是 task_id"). min_out_shares = 0 per architect Q5 MVP. Predecessor TB-N2 B2 R2 hash 31210690 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N2 B2 R2 race fix (2026-05-11; Codex G2 R1 Q8 VETO closure): both EventResolve emit hooks (full-proof ~line 2823 + per-tactic ~line 3515) now NEST inside the existing `if let Some(vid) = verify_tx_id.clone()` block (was: emit happened unconditionally AFTER the block). New call shape passes `&vid` as the 3rd arg to `tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize` so the adapter helper can poll `claims_t[claim_id].status == Finalized` before EventResolve construction (prevents stale `parent_state_root` → StaleParent L4.E). Nesting also tightens the semantics: B2 emit only fires on a real OMEGA-Confirm + FinalizeReward path; previously the emit fired even on paths without `verify_tx_id` (no-op via adapter's `task_markets_t.state != Open` early-out, but semantically loose). Predecessor TB-N2 B2 R1 hash 09af8844 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N2 B2 R2 race fix (2026-05-11; Codex G2 R1 Q8 VETO closure): both EventResolve emit hooks (full-proof ~line 2823 + per-tactic ~line 3515) now NEST inside the existing `if let Some(vid) = verify_tx_id.clone()` block (was: emit happened unconditionally AFTER the block). New call shape passes `&vid` as the 3rd arg to `tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize` so the adapter helper can poll `claims_t[claim_id].status == Finalized` before EventResolve construction (prevents stale `parent_state_root` → StaleParent L4.E). Nesting also tightens the semantics: B2 emit only fires on a real OMEGA-Confirm + FinalizeReward path; previously the emit fired even on paths without `verify_tx_id` (no-op via adapter's `task_markets_t.state != Open` early-out, but semantically loose). Predecessor TB-N2 B2 R1 hash 09af8844 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N2 B2 (2026-05-11; charter §3 B2 + gap audit §3.3 closure): added 2 NEW EventResolve emit hooks in OMEGA-Confirm exit paths (full-proof ~line 2823 + per-tactic ~line 3515). Each constructs `b2_task_id = TaskId(format!("task-{run_id}"))` and calls `turingosv4::runtime::adapter::tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize(&bundle.sequencer, b2_task_id, 5000)` after `tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify`. Best-effort poll-then-emit; failures non-fatal. Closes CPMM lifecycle gap: pre-B2 `TaskMarketState::Finalized` WRITE 0 sites; post-B2 OMEGA-Confirm path flips Open → Finalized making TB-13 CompleteSetRedeem reachable. Predecessor TB-N1 A4 hash ced49f42 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N1-AGENT-ECONOMY Phase 2 A4 (2026-05-10; session #36; charter §2 atom A4 + forward §8 grant): added `verify_peer` tool dispatch arm in `match action.tool.as_str()` between "post" and "step". Reads `action.target_work_tx_id` (required), `action.verdict` ("confirm"/"deny"/missing-defaults-Confirm), `action.bond_micro` (with A3-style saturating `i64::try_from(u).unwrap_or(i64::MAX)` cast + env fallback `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_VERIFY_BOND_MICRO` defaults 1000). Calls `make_real_verifytx_signed_by` and submits via `bus.submit_typed_tx`. FAIL-CLOSED `std::process::exit(3)` on q_snapshot or signing failure; FAIL-OPEN warn-and-skip in legacy mode (no chaintape). Closes the verify-peer agency layer per CLAUDE.md §13 verify/bond + Art. I.1.1 multi-agent verification. Predecessor TB-N1-A3 R2 hash afde6670 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N1-AGENT-ECONOMY Phase 2 A3 R2 Codex Q4 fix (2026-05-10; session #36; PRE-§8 dual audit R1 Codex CHALLENGE Q4): 3 OMEGA callsites' `u as i64` casts replaced with `i64::try_from(u).unwrap_or(i64::MAX)` saturating cast. Pre-fix `u as i64` would WRAP NEGATIVE if agent submitted `stake_micro > i64::MAX` (≈ 9.2 quintillion μC; theoretically possible via JSON injection though no real agent will), routing to `StakeInsufficient` (Step-4a `<= 0`) instead of the intended `StakeBalanceExceeded` (Step-4b `> balance`). Saturating-to-i64::MAX preserves fail-closed admission: u64 overflow → i64::MAX which always exceeds any agent balance (max realistic 30M μC) → fires StakeBalanceExceeded as Codex Q4 demanded. Pure-defensive fix; no protocol-wire-shape change; no sequencer change. Predecessor TB-N1-A3 R1 hash bc016070 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N1-AGENT-ECONOMY Phase 2 A3 (2026-05-10; session #36; charter `handover/tracer_bullets/TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_PHASE_2_charter_2026-05-10.md` §2 atom A3 + forward §8 grant `handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_PHASE_2_FORWARD_§8_GRANT.md`): threaded `action.stake_micro: Option<u64>` through the 3 OMEGA WorkTx-construction callsites (atom2 ~2329, atom3-omega-full ~2645, atom3-omega-pertactic ~3217). Each callsite now reads `action.stake_micro` first; if Some(u), uses that as i64; else falls back to the existing `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PROPOSAL_STAKE_MICRO` env default (1000 μC). Closes the agency layer of CLAUDE.md §13: agent's `step` action carries an explicit per-tx stake within `[1, balance]` instead of the uniform env default. Pairs with sequencer step-4 admission gate + `TransitionError::StakeBalanceExceeded` tail-append to fail-closed reject `stake > balance` with distinct rejection-class telemetry. Predecessor TB-N1-A2 hash 60f41bc8 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N1-AGENT-ECONOMY A2 (2026-05-10; session #35; preflight `handover/alignment/N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_LANDING_GAP_2026-05-10_session35.md`): replaced `let prompt_balance: f64 = ...` lookup at the per-tx prompt build site with `let econ_position: String = ... render_econ_position(&q, &agent_id) ...` invocation per A2 prompt-block landing. New helper at `src/sdk/econ_position.rs` reads canonical EconomicState (balances_t / stakes_t / claims_t / reputations_t) for the agent and returns a structured body that `build_agent_prompt` embeds under `=== Your Economic Position ===` heading. Closes architect ruling §5 Phase 2 typed-PartialAccepted requirement (parent ruling: `handover/directives/2026-05-06_TB18R_ROUND_2_ARCHITECT_RULING.md` §4 Q-P2; FC-first: `handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md` §2.4 Option B). Sequencer's R3 admission helper `refine_rejection_class_via_attempt_telemetry` reads AttemptTelemetry.outcome via WorkTx.proposal_cid and routes to L4.E with refined RejectionClass ∈ {LeanFailed=6, ParseFailed=7, SorryBlocked=8, LlmError=9}. step_partial_ok stays CAS-only per preflight §1.3 amended (LeanPass-on-rejection-arm would trip debug-build panic; intermediate L4 lane deferred to R5+). Variable carries `#[allow(unused_mut, unused_assignments)]` because today no halt path mutates it (today the bundle block is reached only on natural MaxTxExhausted exit per evaluator.rs:3504-3505 comments) — Atom A future-binding will mutate it on DegradedLLM halts. Removed unused `ExhaustionReason` + `RunOutcome` imports from the bundle-block use clause (line 3562) since literals are gone. Regression test at tests/tb_18_evidence_capsule_outcome_propagation.rs (3 tests: literal-scan zero matches for RunOutcome::MaxTxExhausted; ExhaustionReason::MaxTxExhausted appears EXACTLY once = function-header default; .to_run_outcome() projection contract for all 5 ExhaustionReason variants). Closes SG-18.3 + Art.I.2 reproducibility risk that downstream consumers (TB-15 Lamarckian Autopsy + TB-16 Markov inheritance) read the outcome field as ground truth. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 hash 5dfd5142 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual external audit R2 closure): Codex R2 OVERALL CHALLENGE/ship-clean (all 4 R1 VETOs closed; only doc-drift residual). Gemini R2 OVERALL VETO on Q1 (architectural enforcement gap) + Q2 (degenerate smoke). Conservative resolution per feedback_dual_audit_conflict (VETO>CHALLENGE>PASS): file OBS_R024 + TB-17 PRE-17.5 prerequisite (sequencer-side Boltzmann enforcement gate, Class 4 surface, deferred per umbrella charter §0 Class 3 envelope respect + project_tb11_to_tb17_roadmap phase boundary). Q2 closed in r4 (BOLTZMANN_SEED=12345 → distribution {iter-0:1, iter-1:2} non-trivial). This commit's source change: stale-comment cleanup ONLY (no functional code change) — evaluator.rs:1241-1250 docblock above the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS env-var check now mentions "no fallback (since .fix r1)" and "non-None Shannon entropy ≥ 0.5" instead of the pre-fix-r1 wording about fallback to produced_worktx_ids.last() and 0.25 threshold. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental hash 346a6a3c superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental (2026-05-05; preseed-settle barrier added BEFORE the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS iter loop): r2 smoke surfaced a race that the per-iter Codex CHALLENGE #2 fix (removing pre-iter await) inadvertently exposed. The preseed phase queues TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED seed via bus.submit_typed_tx but does NOT block on their commits. Without a settle barrier, iter=0's q_snapshot can fall in the middle of the preseed queue → iter-0 WorkTx parent_state_root captured before another preseed tx commits → apply_one rejects iter-0 with StaleParent → iter-0 lands on L4.E → iter-1's snap.price_index empty → v2_pick=None for iter-1 (the r2 smoke shape: tx_kind_counts.work=3 / l4e_count=2 / iter-1 v2_pick=None instead of expected Some(iter-0)). Fix: poll q_snapshot at start of FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS block until state_root is stable for one poll cycle (preseed queue drained); 50 × 200ms = 10s budget; FAIL-CLOSED if barrier doesn't settle (signals wedged queue). This matches the "settle then dispatch" pattern that the original (commit b5118fd) hook accidentally got via the per-iter pre-await — the per-iter pre-await was structurally on the wrong side of submit (Codex CH2) but it ALSO accidentally drained the preseed queue before iter 0's parent_state_root capture. The settle barrier is the principled replacement. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (without barrier) hash fada36b4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual-audit closure of Codex VETO #1..#4 + CHALLENGE #1..#4 + Gemini Q1/Q2/Q5/Q7/Q8/Q12 CHALLENGES on parent commit b5118fd): (V2 Codex VETO #2 — produced_worktx_ids.push moved to AFTER tb8_await_state_root_advance success; bus.submit_typed_tx is async per src/bus.rs:136-138, so push-before-commit could register a queued tx_id that subsequently failed dispatch). (V4 Codex VETO #4 — env-var parse + CAS / ProposalTelemetry / WorkTx-construction / submit / commit-await failures all converted from warn-and-skip to FAIL-CLOSED via std::process::exit(3); for an explicit user-activated arena hook, partial-success silently biasing the entropy distribution is unacceptable). (CH2 Codex CHALLENGE #2 — pre-iteration tb8_await_state_root_advance was placed BEFORE the tx it was supposed to observe (helper contract per src/runtime/adapter.rs:570-576 says "submit, then await POST-submit"); fix removes the pre-await and only retains the post-submit await per iteration so the next iter's snapshot reflects the prior commit). (CH4 Codex CHALLENGE #4 — removed produced_worktx_ids.last() fallback when v2_pick is None; strict semantics now: parent_tx tracks the v2 selector's authoritative pick or None for root, never a manufactured edge from local state). (V4 supplemental — sequencer.q_snapshot Err converted to FAIL-CLOSED exit). Smoke script `run_tb_16_x_2_4_smoke_2026-05-05.sh` companion fixes (V3): set -euo pipefail (was -uo); explicit RC capture via `|| RC=$?` + abort on RC ≠ 0; refuse to overwrite populated PROBLEM_DIR without ALLOW_REUSE=1; audit_tape stdout captured into AT_LOG variable so pipefail propagates the binary RC. .2.3 + .2.5 smoke scripts also receive the set -e + RC capture carry-over (cheap consistency fix). BOLTZMANN_EPSILON_NUM/DEN env vars (default 9/10) injected so the v2 selector's epsilon-greedy uniform-random branch fires 90% of the time on iter 1+ (vs 10% default), producing diverse parent_tx instead of always-iter-0 lex-tiebreak. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4 hash 5a989d15 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.4 — Multi-WorkTx + Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS=<staker>:<count>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional .2.5 bankruptcy-after-accepted seed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects N (≥3) real-signed WorkTxs serially. Each iteration: (1) snapshot bus → call boltzmann_select_parent_v2(snap.price_index, snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut seeded_rng) — the existing TB-14 Atom 5 integer-rational scheduler (sdk/actor.rs:57); (2) parent_tx = v2 pick OR fallback to most recent produced WorkTx tx_id (so iter 1+ has SOME parent; iter 0 is genuinely root); (3) build + write ProposalTelemetry to CAS via build_for_evaluator_append_with_parent(parent_tx) (mandatory for audit assertion id=24 chain integrity per .2.5 r2 lesson); (4) construct real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) referencing the telemetry CID as proposal_cid; (5) submit + await commit; (6) capture tx_id for next iteration's fallback. Closes the missing R3 "Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise" gap: prior chains had at most 1 admitted WorkTx per task, so the v2 selector had no candidate set to choose from. Audit assertion id=43 (boltzmann_parent_selection_diversity, Layer E supplemental, added in .2.5 commit) verifies Shannon entropy ≥ 0.25 across same-task ProposalTelemetry.parent_tx distribution. DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.4 said "verify boltzmann_select_parent_v2 is called in WorkTx admission path; STEP_B-PROTOCOL TRIGGERED". Position taken: STEP_B refers to sequencer-side dispatch path; v2 selector is ALREADY called proposal-side at evaluator.rs:1828 (captured into _v2_canonical_pick, currently unused). For SG-16.x.2.4 (≥3 WorkTx with parent_selection_entropy ≥ 0.5), the parent_tx record lives in ProposalTelemetry (CAS object) — proposal-time data, not sequencer-side admission data. No sequencer.rs touch needed; STEP_B not triggered. Class 3 dual external audit (Codex + Gemini) STILL applied because the surface is high-impact (Boltzmann RUNTIME wire-up is the V3L-14 anti-star-topology mechanism; Art II.2.1 alarm threshold gate). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r3 hash d39c67d1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 r3 (2026-05-05; smoke r2 surfaced audit assertion id=24 (proposal_telemetry_chain) HALT — the seed WorkTx's proposal_cid was computed via Cid::from_content of a literal string but the bytes were never written to CAS, so cas.get(proposal_cid) returned NotFound, halting the audit. Fix: the seed-WorkTx hook now opens CasStore::open(&bundle.cas_path), constructs a minimal ProposalTelemetry via build_for_evaluator_append (which writes proposal_artifact bytes to CAS as a side-effect), then calls proposal_telemetry::write_to_cas to persist the telemetry itself and uses the returned CID as the WorkTx.proposal_cid. ProposalTelemetry parent_tx is None for the .2.5 seed (single-WorkTx scenario; .2.4 will use Boltzmann pick). verification_result_cid is None (no Lean verification on the seed; id=24 only halts on missing telemetry CAS bytes, NOT on missing VerificationResult). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r2 hash c19dfcbe (which broke audit id=24) superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.5 — AutopsyCapsule real-bankruptcy chain): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED=<staker>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects a real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) so stakes_t has ≥1 entry for `task-{run_id}` at the time TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 emits TaskBankruptcyTx (line ~3150). The TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5 hook (sequencer.rs:1374) then calls derive_autopsies_for_bankruptcy(pre_econ, bk, ...) which iterates stakes_t and emits an AgentAutopsyCapsule with loss_reason_class=Bankruptcy + loss_amount = stake.amount. Closes the missing R2 P4 path "WorkTx-accepted → got accepted → then the task went bankrupt". The seeded WorkTx is REAL (real Ed25519 signature via AgentKeypairRegistry, real predicate_results, real stake debit from preseeded balance) — NOT a synthetic-rejection gate (compare make_synthetic_worktx at line ~1086 which uses synthetic_rejection_for_l4e_gate=true → L4.E only). Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 to materialize WorkTx (admit) → TerminalSummary → TaskBankruptcy → AutopsyCapsule in CAS + AutopsyIndex entry in agent_autopsies_t. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.3 hash e1c4d057 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_REDEEM=<owner>:<outcome>:<share_units> env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (parallel to FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE; OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional so it fires on both OMEGA-Confirm success and MaxTxExhausted+FORCE_BANKRUPTCY paths), constructs an agent-signed CompleteSetRedeemTx via make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by + submits via bus.submit_typed_tx. Pairs with TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED (mints provider's YES+NO shares) + TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY (transitions market to Bankrupt → NO wins) to produce a single chain containing CompleteSetMint → TaskBankruptcy → CompleteSetRedeem sequence — closes the missing 6th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 11-of-13 → 12-of-13). DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.3 spec'd 4 env-var parts (owner:event_id:outcome:share); implementation uses 3 (owner:outcome:share_units) — event_id auto-derived from `task-{run_id}` because run_id contains a unix-ms timestamp minted at evaluator entry (run_id.rs:21) and is unpredictable from the smoke script. Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's auto-derive pattern (line ~3154). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix hash 12489ab4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix (2026-05-05; FC1-emit hook placement bug-fix on TB-16.x.2.2 commit 5e32cbf): the original 5e32cbf placed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE in the post-bundle cleanup section reachable ONLY on the MaxTxExhausted exit path (line 2895 P0-A comment + 2902 mark_final_accept). FORCE_CHALLENGER fires on the OMEGA-Confirm success path which early-returns at make_pput before that cleanup. As filed, smoke r2 produced tx_kind_counts.challenge_resolve=0 + id=42 SKIPPED + ship-gate false-positive (grep matched the field name "challenge_resolve" with value 0 + dashboard run_id slug). This fix mirrors the FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE block onto BOTH OMEGA-Confirm exit paths (full-proof @ ~line 2253 + per-tactic @ ~line 2755), each with post-emit tb8_await_state_root_advance because the OMEGA exit drops bundle without bundle.shutdown drain (evaluator.rs:2936-2938). Original commit's claim "two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive" remains accurate — but the OMEGA path had NO cleanup section at all. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash 993452e4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE=1 env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (deliberately OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional, since FORCE_CHALLENGER fires only on the OMEGA-Confirm success path and the two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive), calls tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t=0, default_resolution=Released) so every Open ChallengeCase is immediately eligible. Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER on the same arena profile to produce a single chain containing Challenge → ChallengeResolve parent-child relationship — closes the missing 5th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 10-of-13 → 11-of-13). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.1 hash eb718a23 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.1 (2026-05-04; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.1 — TaskExpire env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_EXPIRE=1 env-var: after MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule write + (optional) FORCE_BANKRUPTCY emit, calls tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible(seq, current_logical_t=next_logical_t_peek(), expiry_delta_logical_t=0) so every Open/Bankrupt task with elapsed > 0 emits a TaskExpireTx (ExpireReason::Deadline for Open; ExpireReason::BankruptcyTriggered when FORCE_BANKRUPTCY also fired). Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's tb8_await_state_root_advance(pre_ex_root, 5000) prelude so prior commits land before the q_snapshot read. Closes the missing 4th system-emitted tx kind in R3 Round 2 chain (raises 9-of-13 → 10-of-13). Predecessor TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 hash 729a4c5e superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 (2026-05-04; architect §7.3 FR-16.3 + FR-16.4 + FR-16.7): ADDITIVE arena driver hooks. (1) TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER=<agent_id> env-var: after VerifyTx OMEGA-Confirm and before FinalizeReward emit, the named agent submits a real-signed ChallengeTx targeting the WorkTx (FR-16.3). (2) TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED=<provider>:<amount_micro> env-var: after preseed EscrowLock, the named provider submits MarketSeedTx + CompleteSetMintTx against the task's EventId (FR-16.4). (3) TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 env-var: after MAX_TX exhaustion + TerminalSummary emit, sequencer.emit_system_tx(TaskBankruptcy{...}) fires referencing the same EvidenceCapsule (FR-16.7 → autopsy emission via TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5). All three are env-var-gated; no behavior change to existing user-task / preseed paths. Predecessor TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up hash 3530a2d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up (2026-05-03; architect ruling step 1 — Codex R1 primary VETO closure): the canonical TxId returned by `boltzmann_select_parent_v2` MUST NOT flow into legacy shadow `bus.append` parent_id (kernel.tape uses a different (shadow) id namespace; canonical TxId becomes a dangling citation → kernel.append rejects). Per architect ruling: "Use None unless a real shadow id exists." No canonical → shadow id mapping is currently available; pass None (legacy default). The v2 selector still runs (its result captured as `_v2_canonical_pick` for observability + future canonical wire-up — see B′ step 4 CanonicalNodeGraph + parent_tx replacement for last_tx_by_agent), but its output is explicitly NOT passed to bus.append. Closes Codex R1 primary VETO defect #1.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 (2026-05-03; closing OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE): production wire-swap. (1) Imports updated: `BoltzmannParams + boltzmann_select_parent` (legacy f64) → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2 + BoltzmannMaskPolicy` (integer-rational). (2) `BusConfig` literals (×2) drop `system_lp_amount` field. (3) `let params = BoltzmannParams::from_env()` → `let policy = BoltzmannMaskPolicy::from_env()`. (4) Tick-time logging derives `market_count` + top-N price ticker from `bus.snapshot().price_index` (cross-multiplication argmax; rendered `n/d`, never decimal). (5) Per-tx prompt build derives `market_ticker_str` from `snap.price_index` + queries `prompt_balance` from `bus.sequencer.q_snapshot().economic_state_t.balances_t` (replaces legacy `snap.get_balance`; the `f64` is the `prompt.rs` render contract — `prompt.rs` is not a TB-14 module surface). (6) Boltzmann selector call replaced: `boltzmann_select_parent(&snap.tape, &prices, &params, &mut rng)` → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2(&snap.price_index, &snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut rng).map(|tx| tx.0)`. Predicate-blind (CR-14.1 + halt-trigger #1) — purely a scheduling priority pick. Predecessor TB-12 Atom 0.5(a) hash 8f1b97f1 superseded. (2026-05-03; architect 2026-05-03 ruling §1.1 + §8 Atom 0.5; TB-11 G3/G4 carry-forward closure): added 4 EvidenceCapsule counters (tb11_lean_error_count / tb11_sorry_block_count / tb11_protocol_parse_failure_count / tb11_partial_accept_count) instrumented at existing classify_lean_error / classify_parse_error / step_partial_ok call sites; sorry-block vs lean-error split via err_detail.contains("sorry") || err_detail.contains("forbidden_payload"); MAX_TX exhausted exit point (inside `if let Some(bundle) = chaintape_bundle`, BEFORE bundle.shutdown) now writes EvidenceCapsule to CAS via runtime::evidence_capsule::write_evidence_capsule + emits TerminalSummary on-chain via runtime::adapter::tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run with capsule_cid; bundle.shutdown drains the queued TerminalSummary tx via apply_one. Mirror of TB-8 tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify pattern. Predecessor TB-10 Atom 1+3 hash a5b30cd1 superseded.

218:"src/runtime/mod.rs" = "f72bbda70756958c83f5873b3b75585196e7ce821f82e0ca65de32f60f244875"  # rehashed by TB-G G1.1 (2026-05-11 session #40; Class-4 STEP_B; architect §8 SIGNED "好，确认可以 ship" 2026-05-11 session #39 close — canonical Class-4 §8 form; packet §2): added `RuntimeChaintapeConfig.resume_existing_chain: bool` field + strict env gate `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME == "1"` in `from_env` (matches!(Ok("1")) — no truthy-string footgun per packet §5 Q4) + resume branch in `build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q` (when `resume_existing_chain && head_commit_oid().is_some()`: invokes new private helper `bootstrap_resume_state` which reads `pinned_pubkeys.json` + `initial_q_state.json` fail-closed if missing, replays every L4 entry via the canonical `replay_full_transition` FC2 Boot primitive shared with `verify_chaintape`, generates a NEW keypair for a NEW epoch `max_existing_epoch + 1`, appends to manifest so prior-epoch entries still verify, returns `(new_keypair, new_epoch, pinned, replayed_q, chain_length)`) + `Sequencer::new_at_logical_t(seed_q, chain_length)` call so the strict `len + 1` invariant holds on the next commit + new private helper `decode_pubkey_hex_32`. Default `resume_existing_chain: false` preserves the original TB-6 `BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo` fail-closed gate (SG-G1.4 back-compat regression). 5 SG-G1.* tests in `tests/constitution_g1_resume.rs` cover the gates. Constitutional anchor: FC2-Boot §3.2 "every real evidence run must be replayable from genesis_report + ChainTape + CAS + agent registry + system pubkeys" + §4.1 G-009 Path C "replay reconstructs HEAD_t" — resume is the boot-time instance of that replay. Packet §2 adjacent-surfaces row described `head_t_witness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` as the QState-rebuild primitive, but in code that helper only reconstructs the 6-field HeadTWitness derived view from L4/L4.E/CAS ref OIDs (Stage A3 SG-A3.4 derived-view boundary); per FC2 §3.2 + §4.1 the canonical QState replay primitive is `replay_full_transition`, which is what the resume path uses (user 2026-05-11 directive: "关于内核一定要对齐宪法和宪法中的三个flowchart"). Predecessor TB-N3 A2 hash 010db9b5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N3 A2 (2026-05-11; architect ruling 2026-05-11 §8.1+§8.2+§8.6): added `pub mod market_decision_trace;` declaration for the agent invest decision audit trail (NoTradeReason taxonomy + MarketDecisionTrace CAS object + write_market_decision_trace_to_cas helper). Pure additive runtime module exposed under `turingosv4::runtime::market_decision_trace::*`; no chain side effects; consumed by evaluator's TB-N3 A2 invest dispatch arm and audit_dashboard --run-report §F. Predecessor tamper-3-of-3 hash 8cde3e8a superseded.  # rehashed by tamper-3-of-3 fix 2026-05-10 session #33 (M0 batch surfaced TB-16-era drift): added `pub mod audit_tamper;` declaration for the corrected tamper-detection primitives (reachability-aware L4 blob selection + dual-ref truncation). Constitutional Justification: architect §B.9.3 mandate "prove no fake accepted" via 3/3 tamper detection; Stage A3 multi-ref ChainTape (refs/chaintape/{l4,l4e,cas}) invalidated TB-16-era tamper assumptions; M0 P01 evidence showed 1/3. Pure additive runtime module + binary refactor; no chain side effects; exercised by `tests/constitution_audit_tamper_3_of_3.rs`. Per `feedback_no_workarounds_strict_constitution`.
