220-"src/runtime/bootstrap.rs" = "3663f9c861f6f6ebe2a97919c8ea04d24671af23e4ebdb49a94322311fcbb98f"  # rehashed by TB-N3 A0.5 (2026-05-11; architect ruling Q1+Q2+amendment 6 + TB-N3 charter v3 §0.6): added `MarketMakerBudget` 13th preseed entry with 5_000_000 micro (10× safety margin over architect Q1's recommended DEFAULT_POOL_SEED = 100_000 micro × ~5 accepted WorkTx per 9-problem batch = 500_000 micro draw). MarketMakerBudget identity is consumed by TB-N3 A3 `tb_n3_emit_node_market_after_work_accept` adapter helper which signs TaskOpen/MarketSeed/CpmmPool tx via canonical agent paths (no system-emit bypass; no ghost liquidity). 222:"src/runtime/adapter.rs" = "a84afccf67963ae22788f111d23332221d0a7ed6b65a89a96b5e32c68b7aa5d4"  # rehashed by TB-N3 A3 (2026-05-11; Class-4 STEP_B; architect ruling 2026-05-11 amendments 3-6 + Q1+Q2+Q6): added 2 NEW pub items — `NodeMarketEmitOutcome` enum (5 variants: BudgetExhausted / AlreadyExists / Created{event_id, pool_seed_micro} / SubmitFailed{step, message} / PollTimeout{step}) + `tb_n3_emit_node_market_after_work_accept` async helper (mirrors tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize poll-then-emit pattern). Behavior: snapshot Q → idempotency check (cpmm_pools_t[event_id] present → AlreadyExists) → MarketMakerBudget balance precheck (< seed_micro → BudgetExhausted; fail-closed per architect §3.4 + §7 forbidden "ghost liquidity") → sign+submit TaskOpenTx(sponsor=MarketMakerBudget, task=node_survive:work_tx_id) → tb8_await_state_root_advance → sign+submit MarketSeedTx(provider=MarketMakerBudget, event_id, collateral=seed_micro) → await → sign+submit CpmmPoolTx(provider=MarketMakerBudget, event_id, seed_yes=seed_no=seed_micro as u128) → await → Created{event_id, pool_seed_micro}. event_id constructed via node_survive_event_id (architect amendment 1). Architect amendment 4: caller MUST invoke ONLY after WorkTx L4-accepted (no L4.E auto-pool). Architect amendment 3: all 3 sub-tx route via canonical Sequencer::submit_agent_tx admission — no emit_system_tx bypass; no hand-written treasury debit / pool reserve mutation. Best-effort contract: failures return typed NodeMarketEmitOutcome variant; Err reserved for EmitSystemError (currently unreachable since this helper does not touch emit_system_tx — kept for symmetry with sibling helpers). Predecessor TB-N3 A2 hash 2a2f3866 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N3 A2 + A3 prep (2026-05-11; architect ruling 2026-05-11 amendments 1+3+Q5): added 3 NEW pub items — `InvestRouteError` enum (7 variants: ZeroAmount / NegativeAmount / AmountExceedsBalance / MalformedNode / UnknownEvent / PoolNotActive / KeypairError) with `to_no_trade_reason()` mapping into the §8.2 taxonomy + `public_summary()` for the trace.reason_summary_public bound; `tb_n3_invest_to_router_tx` (sync constructor; pre-classifies via optional snapshot then signs canonical BuyWithCoinRouterSigningPayload via AgentKeypairRegistry::sign; mirrors make_real_*_signed_by shape; min_out_shares = 0 per architect Q5 MVP; event_id constructed via node_survive_event_id per amendment 1); `make_real_cpmm_pool_signed_by` (sync; mirrors make_real_market_seed_signed_by shape; symmetric init; required by forthcoming A3 emit helper for canonical signing path per amendment 3 "A3 应优先用 MarketSeedTx ... 不得手写 treasury debit / pool reserve"). Predecessor TB-N2 B2 R2 hash 005e1efb superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N2 B2 R2 race fix (2026-05-11; Codex G2 R1 Q8 VETO closure): `tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize` now accepts `verify_tx_id: &TxId` 3rd param and derives `claim_id = TxId(format!("claim-{verify_tx_id}"))` (mirrors tb8 helper's claim_id_inner at adapter.rs:622). Poll loop now requires BOTH `claims_t[claim_id].status == ClaimStatus::Finalized` AND `task_markets_t[task_id].state == Open` before emit. The claim Finalized witness signals FinalizeReward dispatch arm has applied (state_root advanced past R_0), so the subsequent `emit_system_tx` captures the post-FinalizeReward state_root → no StaleParent L4.E race. Prior R1 helper polled only task_markets_t (which is unchanged by FinalizeReward); smoke evidence `handover/evidence/stage_b3_smoke_b2_20260511T012401Z/deepseek-v4-flash/seed1/rep1/P002_aime_1983_p2/runtime_repo/rejections.jsonl:9` showed `tx_kind:EventResolve public_summary:stale_parent_root` — the B2 transition was rejected at apply time, leaving task_markets_t.state unchanged at Open and the CPMM lifecycle gap §3.3 not actually closed. R2 fix is binding-tested by `tests/constitution_n2_event_resolve.rs::sg_n2_b2_9_adapter_polls_claim_finalized_before_emit_r2`. Predecessor TB-N2 B2 R1 hash 18bc199e superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N2 B2 (2026-05-11): added `tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize` poll-then-emit helper (mirrors `tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify` pattern). Polls `q_snapshot` until task_markets_t[task_id].state == Open; emits `SystemEmitCommand::EventResolve` via `Sequencer::emit_system_tx`. Best-effort contract: Ok(true) on emit / Ok(false) on poll-budget-expiry or task already resolved / Err on unexpected emit_system_tx error. Closes the CPMM lifecycle automation gap from the OMEGA-Confirm exit path. Predecessor hash 48da399a superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE — added make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by(keypairs, parent_state_root, event_task, owner, outcome, share_units, suffix, timestamp_logical) helper. Mirrors make_real_complete_set_mint_signed_by shape: builds CompleteSetRedeemTx + canonical-digest + agent-sign via AgentKeypairRegistry. Sole caller is the FORCE_REDEEM env-var hook in evaluator.rs (TB-16.x.2.3); no production caller wired yet. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 hash c1360a73 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 (2026-05-05; doc-only follow-up addressing Codex CHALLENGE E.3 from the .fix Class 3 audit): the prior .fix Patch E docstring referenced a nonexistent `Sequencer::set_current_round_for_test` symbol while explaining the time-unit difference between this helper and tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible. Patch F4 corrects the doc to point at the actual in-tree mutator (the `seed_q_for_challenge` test helper at src/state/sequencer.rs:~4185) and clarifies that no production round-advance mechanism is wired yet. NO behavior change — pure docstring edit; helper signature, eligibility logic, and call surface are unchanged from .fix Patch E. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash a9bbb1ac superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE — added tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t, default_resolution) helper. Mirrors tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible shape: scan economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t for entries with status=Open AND q.q_t.current_round - case.opened_at_round > window_delta; emit ChallengeResolveTx via emit_system_tx (SystemEmitCommand::ChallengeResolve) for each eligible case. Returns (count, bonds_released_micro). emit_system_tx for ChallengeResolve does NOT pre-check case existence/status (sequencer.rs:2590 — construction is unconditional); dispatch-time AlreadyResolved/ChallengeNotFound surfaces as L4.E rejection, not Err here. Predecessor TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 hash 3390205f superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 (2026-05-04; architect §7.3 FR-16.3 + FR-16.4): ADDITIVE — added 3 real-signature constructors for arena drivers: make_real_challengetx_signed_by (ChallengeTx; FR-16.3), make_real_market_seed_signed_by (MarketSeedTx; FR-16.4), make_real_complete_set_mint_signed_by (CompleteSetMintTx; FR-16.4). Mirror existing make_real_worktx_signed_by / make_real_verifytx_signed_by patterns. No behavior change to existing helpers. ./handover/evidence/tb_18_minif2f_m1_2026-05-05T18-31-55Z/M1_RUN_MANIFEST.json:86:  "anti_drift_contract": "Manifest is FROZEN at batch start. If any field changes mid-batch (e.g. model version drift, mathlib re-fetch, problem list rotation), batch is INVALIDATED and must restart with new manifest (new created_at_utc + new turingosv4_commit + new manifest_id). Drift detection: every Nth-problem checkpoint cross-checks model_version + mathlib_commit + turingosv4_commit; mismatch halts batch, records the drift event, requires new manifest before resume.",

./handover/evidence/tb_18_minif2f_m1_2026-05-05T18-31-55Z/README.md:10:> Per-LLM-call externalization was **not yet enforced** at this run. Failure-path asymmetry is present: success ω-paths externalize correctly, but `step_reject` / `parse_fail` / `llm_err` / `step_partial_ok` paths leak to evaluator stdout / kernel.tape shadow without L4 / L4.E entries. P49-class heavy runs show `evaluator_tx_count=32` but `L4_WorkTx=1` and `L4.E_real_LLM_rejection=0`.

./handover/evidence/tb_18_minif2f_m1_2026-05-05T18-31-55Z/MINIF2F_M1_BENCHMARK_REPORT.md:7:> Per-LLM-call externalization was **not yet enforced** at this run. Failure-path asymmetry is present: success ω-paths externalize correctly, but `step_reject` / `parse_fail` / `llm_err` / `step_partial_ok` paths leak to evaluator stdout / kernel.tape shadow without L4 / L4.E entries. P49-class heavy runs show `evaluator_tx_count=32` but `L4_WorkTx=1` and `L4.E_real_LLM_rejection=0`.

./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3493:### Markov capsule = None per FC2 Boot + Markov chain genesis (NOT a workaround)
./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3498:1. **MarkovEvidenceCapsule is NOT a flowchart node.** FC1 (`constitution.md:455-509`, anti-oreo runtime loop), FC2 (`constitution.md:571-660`, Boot/Init), and FC3 (`constitution.md:826-870`, meta-architecture) contain no Markov capsule node. The capsule is a TB-15 派生视图 (CR-15.5: "evidence compression, not hidden source of truth — every field is derivable from the chain + CAS").
./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3499:2. **Markov chain genesis is `previous_capsule_cid: None`** (`src/runtime/markov_capsule.rs:60` + line 111). A fresh isolated chain has no inherited Markov by definition.
./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3500:3. **TB-16.x.2.1's smoke run is constitutionally a genesis chain**: fresh `runtime_repo` + fresh `cas`, no `previous_capsule_cid` anywhere in its bytes. Therefore `markov_capsule = None` is the **only** constitutionally correct state per FC2 Boot semantic + Markov chain definition. The 7 Layer G `Skipped` outcomes are CORRECT, not bypassed.
./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3501:4. **`audit_tape --markov-pointer <absent path>` is the public API for "no inherited Markov"** (`src/runtime/audit_assertions.rs:421-425`'s `if pointer.exists() else None` branch). The smoke runner's choice to pass `/tmp/tb16x21_no_markov_pointer.txt` is the genesis-chain expression of that API — NOT a workaround.
./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3505:`handover/markov_capsules/LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` is an Art. 0.2 平行账本 (parallel ledger) — a global filesystem pointer whose target cid's bytes live in exactly ONE per-problem CAS (TB-16 R3 Round 2 P8_completeset_b's). It carries cross-chain provenance with no 守恒 test, no derivability from any tape, no architectural anchoring. **Pre-TB-16.x.1 silently masked this with `.ok()` collapse → false-PROCEED Skipped.** TB-16.x.1's fix correctly fail-closes the masking. The remaining Art. 0.2 violation is the **existence of the global pointer itself**, not anything sub-atom 2.x evidence could fix locally.

./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3931:> TB-15 establishes Lamarckian Autopsy + Markov EvidenceCapsule substrate. AgentAutopsyCapsule (per-agent, per-event, AuditOnly) records loss/bankruptcy events derived deterministically from ChainTape evidence — NEVER LLM self-narration. agent_autopsies_t lives sequencer-side (NOT projected to AgentVisibleProjection per CR-15.1 + halt-trigger #1). TypicalErrorBroadcast clustering at N≥3 emits public_summary text + Cids only — NEVER private_detail_cid bytes. MarkovEvidenceCapsule binds constitution_hash + L4 + L4.E + CAS roots + previous capsule + typical_errors + unresolved_obs as next-session bootstrap default; deeper history requires `TURINGOS_MARKOV_OVERRIDE=1`. ./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:4396:"TB-13 introduces the Polymarket / CTF mathematical core (`1 locked Coin = 1 YES_E + 1 NO_E`) as a non-trading collateral + share accounting layer on top of TB-12's NodePositionsIndex substrate. CompleteSetMintTx is balance↔collateral migration with equal YES/NO claim issuance; CompleteSetRedeemTx redeems winning side post-system-resolved outcome (canonical `task_markets_t.state`); MarketSeedTx provider explicit-funds protocol-owned share inventory. Six-holding CTF (balances + escrows + stakes + challenge_cases + conditional_collateral) preserved bit-equal across all 3 typed-tx; conditional shares are claims, NOT Coin (CR-13.3 + SG-13.2). MIN-semantics `assert_complete_set_balanced` invariant called live from each dispatch arm post-mutation. Submit-time + replay-time agent signature verification (Class 3 admission control) for all 3 variants. Forward-fence (3-layer marker + type-name + hard-import discipline) prevents legacy f64 CPMM contamination. Two architect-spec deviations (`u128 ShareAmount` + `ResolutionRef` removed) endorsed by clean-room auditor as tighter-than-spec but requiring architect ratification before TB-14."

./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_v4_WHITEPAPER_v2_2026-04-27_ANTI_OREO_RESTORATION.md:88:TuringOS 的 tape 是一个可验证状态账本。它可以由本地 hash chain、Git-like CAS、permissioned ledger、rollup、public chain settlement 实现。**区块链不是 TuringOS 的核心，反奥利奥才是核心。**

./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_v4_WHITEPAPER_v2_2026-04-27_ANTI_OREO_RESTORATION.md:427:> **L5 vs L6 区分 (Art 0.2 重建性合规)**: Layer 5 是**当前状态的物化视图**（agent 直接 read_tool 读到的最小充分上下文，含 `agent_reputation_index` / `price_signal_index`）。Layer 6 是**统计聚合 / 趋势压缩**（`reputation counters` 是窗口内增量统计，`price signals` 是市场微观结构压缩）。**L6 严格 derivable from L4 + L5**：删掉 L6 不破坏 Art 0.2 重建性，因为 L4 (transition ledger) 是 ground truth，L5 是 L4 物化，L6 是 L5 统计变换。L6 存在的唯一目的是**降低顶层白盒访问成本**（从 O(账本扫描) 降到 O(预聚合查询)）。

./handover/manifests/TB-18_M1_BENCHMARK_MANIFEST.json:76:  "anti_drift_contract": "Manifest is FROZEN at batch start. If any field changes mid-batch (e.g. model version drift, mathlib re-fetch, problem list rotation), batch is INVALIDATED and must restart with new manifest (new created_at_utc + new turingosv4_commit + new manifest_id). Drift detection: every Nth-problem checkpoint cross-checks model_version + mathlib_commit + turingosv4_commit; mismatch halts batch, records the drift event, requires new manifest before resume.",

./handover/whitepapers/REVISION_NOTES_2026-04-27.md:98:**Issue**: line 105 reads "Phase 1: Local Ledger Economy (ledger.jsonl + SQLite + Python predicates)". This was an early Path A vision; ratified Path B uses gix substrate + Rust predicates. WP and code/spec drift = future audit liability.
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:22:⚪ 底层白盒：read/write tools、CAS、sandbox、ledger、权限、执行器
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:57:read tool、write tool、CAS、sandbox、ledger、executor、permission checker 都必须是确定性白盒。工具不解释意图，只执行规则。遇到权限、签名、hash、schema、budget、predicate 不一致时，默认拒绝。
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:71:TuringOS 的 tape 是一个可验证状态账本。它可以由本地 hash chain、Git-like CAS、permissioned ledger、rollup、public chain settlement 实现。区块链不是 TuringOS 的核心，反奥利奥才是核心。
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:143:    ledger
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:185:ledger        决定历史如何被记录
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:221:  ledger_root_t,
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:233:ledger_root_t             历史账本根
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:383:Hyperledger Fabric 文档明确区分 world state 与 blockchain：world state 保存当前值，blockchain 记录导致当前 world state 的全部变化，且写入后不可修改。这个模型非常接近 ChainTape 的"当前 tape 视图 + 不可变历史账本"结构。
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:503:ledger.append(accepted_tx_i)
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:511:ledger.append(rejected_tx_i)
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:679:失败记录可以写入底层 ledger，但不能默认进入 Agent 上下文。
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:682:ledger stores evidence
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:808:ledger writes genesis block
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:942:append-only ledger.jsonl
./handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_WHITEPAPER_v1_2026-04-26.md:979:Hyperledger Fabric 的 ledger 模型由 world state 与 blockchain 组成，并要求满足背书策略的交易才会更新 world state，这与 TuringOS 的"谓词通过后才能写入状态"高度同构。

./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:9:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:25:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:26:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:27:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:75:| `run_id::mint_run_id` correlation key + 9 wired FC events (A6 + A8e F4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | A6 introduced a per-run correlation id (originally named `run_corr_id`, now unified into `run_id`) anchoring all events. A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) split this out into a dedicated module + threaded the same id into `make_pput` so FC events and v2 jsonl rows share one identifier (no ms drift). Anchor sites: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit / mr tick FC2-N20 / OMEGA full-proof FC2-N22 / OMEGA per-tactic FC2-N22 / natural MaxTxExhausted FC2-N22 (with `budget_regime` payload from A5) / FC1-N12 oneshot verify (A6) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (A8e F4) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_partial` (A8e F4). ./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:76:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |
./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:112:6. **FC2-N23 HaltReason full taxonomy as Rust enum** — currently only OmegaAccepted is typed; other 4 variants live as jsonl strings. Phase C+ Soft Law mode work may force this typing.
./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:155:- `handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md` (FC3-E14 vs FC2-N22 distinction)
./handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:14:This panic is **not** a FC2-N22 HALT, even though the surface effect ("process stops") looks similar. The mapping question matters because TRACE_MATRIX_v1 needed to decide where to attach this code path in the constitutional flowcharts.
./handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:16:## Why TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED ≠ FC2-N22 HALT
./handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:18:| Property | FC2-N22 HALT | TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panic |
./handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:26:FC2 has a `HALT` node (FC2-N22) that lives *inside* the boot/tick lifecycle. TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED fires *before* the boot lifecycle — it is a precondition violation on the readonly base.
./handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:45:2. Adding the variant would lie about the constitutional structure — we would be claiming Trust Root verification is part of the FC2 boot/tick cycle, when it is in fact a precondition for FC2 to begin.
./handover/alignment/OBS_STAGE_A3_R7_GEMINI_R1_FORWARD_BIND_2026-05-08.md:43:Gemini R1 Q2 verbatim concern: "TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH environment variable is an architecturally weak seam... introduces implicit, global state ('action at a distance')... fails in scenarios with multiple concurrent TuringOS instances within a single process".
./handover/alignment/OBS_STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_V1_5_HOUSEKEEPING_2026-04-29.md:111:**FC-trace**: FC3-L4 (downstream supersession sediment for L4 transition ledger family; alignment-only observation, not a behavior change).
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB_16_X_2_2_FIX_EVIDENCE_CAPSULE_HARDCODED_MAXTX_2026-05-05.md:23:The EvidenceCapsule `outcome` field carries semantic load — downstream consumers (TB-15 Lamarckian Autopsy, TB-16 Markov inheritance) read it as ground truth. A capsule labeled `MaxTxExhausted` written for a future code path that legitimately reaches it under, say, `WallClockCap` would silently misrepresent run terminal state — Art. I.2 reproducibility violation propagating through the chain.
./handover/alignment/OBS_M0_DEEPSEEK_DRIFT_2026-05-05.md:96:- ✅ Markov capsule absent (genesis chain, no inheritance)

./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v1_2026-04-25.md:119:2. ~~**TRACE_MATRIX of B7-extra (p_0 calibration toggle)**~~ — landed. Final implementation differs slightly from the original sketch in this section: the constitutional `bus.register_predicate(...)` API does not currently exist on `main` (it lives on the unmerged `phase-z-wtool-tools` branch — TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC1-N11 references it aspirationally). Rather than scope-creep B7-extra into reviving Phase Z, the synthetic predicate is implemented at the evaluator layer in `rollback_sim.rs` with an explicit short-circuit at the threshold tx. The constitutional anchor (FC1-E18 ∏p=0 → Q_t repeated, then FC2-N22 HALT via existing `MaxTxExhausted`) is unchanged; only the abstraction depth differs. Listed under § 2 above as ✅ entries.

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:5:**Amended 2026-04-29 (post-audit)**: external auditor's CF-1 / CF-3 / CF-5 incorporated per `handover/audits/2026-04-29_external_audit.md` and user authorization on 2026-04-29. Specific amendments: Day-3 wording switched to L4 / L4.E split (rejected submissions go to L4.E rejection-evidence ledger, NOT to L4 with `status=rejected`); Day-2 framing sharpened (WalletTool = read-only projection of `EconomicState.balances_t`, not "legacy adapter"); Day-5 acceptance gate downgraded so P1/P3 are blocking and P6 artifacts are non-blocking until RSP-1.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:19:**Original charter**: commit `4ecb708` body. Original GOAL was *"One MiniF2F adaptation problem solved end-to-end at HEAD with the full v4 5-step compile loop active per-tx + economy hooks firing per-tx + L4 ledger commits per-tx + h_vppu computed in PputResult."* That goal bundled four different layer-jumps (P1 ledger, P3 economy, P5 capability compilation, P6 metric) into one 7-day TB.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:72:**WalletTool framing (sharpened post-audit 2026-04-29 CF-3)**: `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is **NOT a legacy mutable adapter** — it is a *read-only projection* of `QState.economic_state_t.balances_t`. Mutations to economic state happen exclusively through the canonical RSP path (`SettlementEngine` / `EscrowVault` / `StakeManager` / `monetary_invariant`). No new RSP code may depend on `WalletTool.credit()` or on `WalletTool` mutating its `HashMap<String, f64>` to represent canonical balance state. Existing `WalletTool` tests stay temporarily as legacy behavior tests; they get removed or rewritten as RSP-1/RSP-2 lands.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:79:**Exit addressed**: P1:5 (state_root advances on accept), P1:6 (state_root unchanged on reject), P1:7 (ledger hash chain), P1:8 (state.db reconstruction), P1:9 (rejected-log isolation)
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:82:**Build** (post-audit 2026-04-29 CF-1: TWO ledgers, not one):
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:83:- `src/economy/ledger.rs` — minimum-viable accepted-only L4 wrapper around the existing `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`. Provides:
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:87:- `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs` (NEW, post-audit 2026-04-29 CF-1): minimum-viable rejection-evidence ledger:
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:92:  - **No `state_root` advance** — `dispatch_transition` rejection path MUST NOT mutate `q.state_root_t` or `q.ledger_root_t`.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:96:  - `test_p1_kill_3_ledger_reconstructable`: drop state.db; reconstruct from L4 only; bit-equal to pre-drop `state_root`. L4.E intentionally not consulted in reconstruction.

./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:7:**Decision class**: architectural boundary, P1 GitTape Kernel ledger semantics
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:14:**Rejected submissions never enter the L4 accepted-transition ledger.**
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:16:The two ledgers are distinct, with distinct identifiers, distinct hash chains, and distinct contribution to `Q_t`:
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:19:L4   Accepted Transition Ledger  (= existing src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs)
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:21:     - hash chain:        ledger_root chain over accepted entries
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:22:     - Q_t contribution:  advances state_root_t and ledger_root_t
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:23:     - replay use:        replay_full_transition reconstructs Q_t from L4 only
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:26:L4.E Rejection Evidence Ledger   (= new src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs)
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:31:     - Q_t contribution:  NONE — does NOT mutate state_root_t or ledger_root_t
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:45:If rejected entries shared `logical_t` with accepted entries, the invariant "every `logical_t` is an accepted state transition; replay produces bit-equal `state_root`" would break. Then `replay_full_transition` would either need to skip `logical_t`s (defeats the monotone-time abstraction) or apply rejected entries as no-ops (defeats the auditability of why a `logical_t` exists at all).
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:47:Splitting the ledgers means:
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:48:- `replay_full_transition` consumes L4 only and produces a deterministic `state_root`.
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:53:Accepted transitions are public constitutional state by definition. Rejected diagnostics are *failure detail*: stack traces, predicate-error strings, raw LLM output. If both lived in the same ledger, every Agent's materialized view would have to choose between (a) seeing all of L4 plus rejected diagnostics — context contamination + Goodhart attack surface, or (b) filtering at read time — which puts privacy logic in `materializer.py` instead of in the ledger schema, where it belongs.
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:61:The constitutional "selective broadcasting / selective shielding" principle (Art. III.4) requires that one Agent's failure does not pollute another Agent's prompt. A single-ledger model would force every read view to scrub rejected entries — an opt-out model that is fragile against future code paths.
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:77:            self.transition_ledger.append(entry)?;
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:78:            // logical_t increments here; state_root + ledger_root advance
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:95:            // CRITICAL: do NOT mutate q.state_root_t or q.ledger_root_t.
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:96:            // Specifically: do NOT call self.transition_ledger.append(...) here.
./handover/alignment/DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md:124:- Whether L4.E is content-addressed (CAS) under-the-hood. Recommendation: yes (each `raw_diagnostic` and each `RejectedSubmissionRecord` payload becomes a CID), but the decision binds only the ledger-separation semantics, not the storage backend.

./handover/alignment/STRICT_AUDIT_TBC0_TAPE_2026-05-07.md:133:| FC3-INV2 no global Markov pointer | ✅ | filesystem invariant — `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` absent | — |
./handover/alignment/STRICT_AUDIT_TBC0_TAPE_2026-05-07.md:159:| FC3-INV1 capsule integrity | **Real-problem path**: run any MiniF2F problem in CONTINUATION mode (`--prior-chain-runtime-repo` flag from `audit_tape_tamper` / `audit_tape`) so a Markov capsule chain exists. The 4 markov_*_recompute assertions then run. | re-use the same batch's runtime_repos as prior_chain on a second run; e.g., `mathd_numbertheory_1124` (which has CAS-resident EvidenceCapsule from this batch) → run any MiniF2F problem with `--prior-chain-runtime-repo` pointing at it |
./handover/alignment/STRICT_AUDIT_TBC0_TAPE_2026-05-07.md:194:The round-4 closure report claimed "Constitution Landing Gate ready for §8 sign-off; 21/25 GREEN". Strict audit says: **the empirical tape evidence supports 17/25 chain-resident GREEN, 7 AMBER (3 of which are bug-blocked, not structural-only), and 1 RED**. The TB-C0 work is REAL — the tests exist, the harness ran, the chain is well-formed and replayable, the economy invariants hold, the predicate routing is correct, the no-global-pointer is enforced. But the closure-candidate claim was over-rosy because:
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:5:**Severity**: constitutional (Art. 0.2 violation, parallel-ledger pattern)
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:13:The file `handover/markov_capsules/LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` is a single-line filesystem-side hex pointer used by `audit_tape --markov-pointer` (`src/runtime/audit_assertions.rs:421`) to resolve the Markov capsule cid that an audit run should validate Layer G against.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:24:(The TB-16 R3 Round 2 P8_completeset_b per-problem isolated CAS — the LAST sub-problem to run `generate_markov_capsule` with `--out-dir` writing the global pointer.)
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:27:- No global / project-root CAS holds them.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:38:- a **filesystem-side global** (not derived from any tape)
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:39:- holding a **cid pointing at bytes resident in exactly one per-problem CAS** (not globally CAS-resolvable)
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:43:This is the **textbook pattern** Art. 0.2 names as 平行账本 (parallel ledger) — it carries information across chains in a way that no single tape can reconstruct.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:47:1. **Cross-tape coupling without provenance**: a `audit_tape` run on tape A, asked to validate against `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` set by tape B's last `generate_markov_capsule` invocation, has no way to verify "is B legitimately my predecessor?" The Markov chain field `previous_capsule_cid: Option<Cid>` (`src/runtime/markov_capsule.rs:60`) **does** encode predecessor relations, but only **inside a capsule's own bytes** — not at the pointer file. The pointer is content-blind.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:48:2. **Hidden source-of-truth**: pre-TB-16.x.1, `read_markov_capsule(...).ok()` collapsed all errors to `None`, masking pointer-CAS mismatches as silent `Skipped`. Round 2 verdict.json shows `markov_constitution_hash_matches: Skipped` — that wasn't legitimate "no prior markov"; it was the masked failure of this very pointer-bytes mismatch. CR-15.6 ("Markov default prevents context poisoning") was bypassed because the pointer's existence was *de facto* the source of truth, not the capsule bytes.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:49:3. **No 守恒 test**: `tests/` has no harness that asserts the global pointer file's cid is derivable from any reference tape. Test `markov_capsule.rs:464+474+732+740` cover capsule round-trip + with_constitution_hash, NOT the pointer file.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:55:But that fix exposed the **deeper** Art. 0.2 violation: a fresh isolated CAS (e.g. TB-16.x.2.1's smoke run on aime_1997_p9) cannot resolve the global pointer's cid, because the bytes only exist in TB-16 R3 Round 2 P8's CAS. Pre-fix, this was masked as "Skipped (no prior markov)". Post-fix, it's correctly surfaced as "this pointer references bytes I cannot verify" — and the operator must explicitly choose pointer-absent semantics to get past it.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:72:Pointer-absent → `markov_capsule = None`. The 7 Layer G assertions then `Skipped` per the standard "no Markov capsule" branch (e.g. `assert_32_markov_constitution_hash_matches` at line 2006). This is the **constitutionally correct outcome for a fresh genesis chain** — the Markov chain definition itself encodes genesis as `previous_capsule_cid: None` (`markov_capsule.rs:111`).
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:74:**TB-16.x.2.1's smoke run is constitutionally a genesis chain**: fresh `runtime_repo` + fresh `cas`, with no `previous_capsule_cid` claim anywhere in its bytes. The smoke runner's choice to pass an absent pointer path is the CORRECT expression of "this chain has no inherited Markov" — it is NOT a workaround for the global-pointer brokenness. The brokenness affects sub-atom 2.x evidence ONLY in that the smoke runner cannot use the global pointer (because resolving it would BLOCK), so the smoke runner reasonably and correctly opts out of any claim to inherited Markov.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:77:- Sub-atom 2.1 LATEST.md called this an "OBS deferred to umbrella sub-atom 2.6 — fix the markov bootstrap" — wrong framing. The per-sub-atom smoke evidence is *correct*; the global pointer is the violation.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:78:- Sub-atom 2.1 README called the absent-path the "workaround" — wrong word. It's the Markov-chain-genesis API.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:89:- **Patch** `generate_markov_capsule` so `--out-dir` writes only PER-RUN markov pointer alongside the per-run capsule json; delete the global write path.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:90:- **Inheritance semantic**: each `audit_tape` invocation declares its prior chain (if any) via a new `--prior-chain-runtime-repo <path>` arg. The capsule cid + bytes are then resolved by reading the prior chain's runtime_repo + traversing its CAS. **This makes the Markov inheritance fully tape-derived (Art. 0.2 守恒 satisfied).**
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:97:- Under such continuation, the prior task's Markov capsule **is in the same CAS** as the current task — no global pointer needed. The Markov inheritance is structurally encoded in `Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩`'s `tape_t` itself.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:107:- **Trade-off**: adds a SECOND parallel-ledger to police the FIRST one. Constitutionally weaker than α / β. Recommended only if chain continuation (β) is multi-month away.
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:122:| `handover/markov_capsules/LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` | global pointer | DELETE | retain as non-canonical |
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:129:| `tests/` | 守恒 test | NEW: derive_pointer_from_tape | NEW: chain-continuation Markov binding |
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:140:- FC2 (Boot/Init) — `constitution.md` lines 571-660
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:144:- Markov chain genesis semantic — `src/runtime/markov_capsule.rs:60+111`
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:147:- TB-16 R3 Round 2 P8_completeset_b (where the global pointer's bytes physically live) — `handover/evidence/tb_16_real_llm_arena_2026-05-04/post_r3_round2/P8_completeset_b/cas/`
./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:155:- New ship gates from ruling: SG-16.7 (no global Markov pointer canonical input) + SG-16.8 (fresh isolated chain → `markov_capsule=None`, Layer G Skipped) + SG-16.9 (present-but-unresolvable Markov pointer BLOCKS) + SG-16.10 (multi-task continuation uses same `runtime_repo`+CAS or explicit `--prior-chain-runtime-repo`).

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:48:Concurrently, the runtime carries a parallel f64 ledger in `WalletTool` (`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs:24`) that pre-dates `EconomicState.balances_t` (introduced CO P2.0). Every dispatch arm since TB-3 has mutated `EconomicState.balances_t` (MicroCoin); the legacy `WalletTool.balances` (f64) is mutated only by `bus.rs` market shares code (the v3 simulation path that ChainTape does not touch). **Two ledgers, one canonical chain** — this is the literal definition of `feedback_proxy_running_vs_source_drift`-style trap waiting for the next reviewer to step on it.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:50:Per architect directive 2026-05-02 ruling 13 + LATEST §9: TB-9 closes both gaps in one bundle. Durable AgentRegistry **and** Wallet Projection are co-located because they share the same invariant — one canonical record, replayable from L4, no shadow ledger.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:67:  WalletTool retains its TuringTool surface but holds zero owned f64 state. balance(agent)
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:69:  bus.rs market f64 mutator path (debit_wallet / credit_wallet / record_shares — all
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:74:The conservation invariant `Σ balances_t + Σ escrows_t = total_supply_micro` is preserved exactly across the WalletTool collapse — Goal B is a **deletion of a parallel ledger that was never load-bearing on the chain path**, not a migration of state. There is no f64 ↔ MicroCoin transfer step.

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:421:**Recommendation**: **single encrypted file** at `~/.turingos/keystore/agent_keystore.enc` containing serialized `BTreeMap<AgentId, AgentSecretBytes>`, mirroring `system_keypair_v{epoch}.enc` precedent exactly. **Reason**: one file = one fsync = one atomic rename; mirrors the existing keystore precedent (`src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:417-463`); no dir-walk for multi-agent runs; KDF/nonce reuse on the encrypted-at-rest layer.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:441:**Recommendation**: **read-only projection wrapper retained** as the TuringTool surface; all f64 owned-state deleted; mutators deleted; bus market path deleted. **Reason**: minimum-blast-radius — keeps the TuringTool trait registration intact for tool-discovery code (`src/bottom_white/tools/registry.rs`); removes parallel ledger; explicit `balance(agent, &EconomicState)` projection signature documents the coupling.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:447:**Recommendation**: **delete** the bus.rs market f64 mutator code (lines ~287-490) entirely along with its tests. **Reason**: this is v3 simulation pre-dating ChainTape (TB-3 onward); zero production binary calls these paths; per `feedback_chaintape_wire_up_priority`, ChainTape is the canonical path; legacy code is debt awaiting amplification.

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-C0_CLOSURE_REPORT_2026-05-06.md:121:**Note on FC3 AMBERs**: per `feedback_real_problems_not_designed` §edge-case clause and `FC_WITNESS_CATALOG_2026-05-06.md` §2.3, these 4 nodes describe meta-architectural roles (raw-log shielding policy, deep-history env-flag, architect-via-directive-trail, judge-via-audit-trail) that **inherently do not run on the tape**. They are verified by structural source-grep tests in `constitution_fc3_meta.rs` + `constitution_shielding_gate.rs` + `constitution_no_parallel_ledger.rs`. There is no real existing problem that could produce a chain-resident witness for "the architect did not directly write src/" — that's a procedural fact, not a runtime event.

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-8_charter_2026-05-02.md:349:If TB-8 ships clean, the highest-value next slot is **TB-9 = Durable AgentRegistry + Wallet Projection**. TB-7R + TB-8 leave the system with run-local agent identity; TB-9 makes it persistent. Without durable identity, future NodeMarket position ownership and TB-10 user-facing payout addresses are not stable. Alongside the durable registry, `WalletTool` collapses to a read-only projection (no f64 mutation; `EconomicState` remains canonical).

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:73:  This is the Art 0.2 守恒测试 contract for derived views (`assert_eq!(view, derive_from_tape(...))`); without it `total_escrow` becomes a parallel ledger.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:250:`L4ERejectionClass` lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs::RejectionClass`. TB-3 adds the `InsufficientBalance` variant + matching `public_summary` arm `"insufficient_balance"`. If `state::typed_tx::RejectionClass` is also extended (used by terminal summary / typed status), the two enums must be kept in sync — Atom 3 audits both.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:258:- **`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`** — `TxKind` enum extension for 2 new variants (cascading match additions across consumers).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:259:- **`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs`** — `RejectionClass` enum extension for `InsufficientBalance`.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:305:2. **No use of `economy::ledger::AcceptedLedger` as the production accepted L4** (TB-2 #6 inherited).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:395:> After Proof 1 setup, a predicate-passing `WorkTx`(task_id=T, agent=Solver, stake=K) where `balances_t[Solver] >= K` is admitted, advances `state_root_t` to `sha256(WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1 ‖ prev ‖ canonical_hash(work))`, advances `ledger_root_t`, increments `logical_t`, debits `balances_t[Solver]` by K, inserts `stakes_t[work.tx_id] = StakeEntry { amount: K, staker: Solver, task_id: T }`. Each missing precondition (no TaskOpen / no EscrowLock / Solver balance < K / stake==0) routes the WorkTx to L4.E with the appropriate class WITHOUT mutating economic_state. The bridge code at `sequencer.rs:205-215` is deleted in the same commit; `bridge_pattern_does_not_resurrect_in_src` test passes. (Tests I23 + I24 + I25 + I26 + I27 + I28.)
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:398:> Reconstructing `QState` from canonical L4 alone (extending TB-2's `replay_full_transition` with the 2 new TxKind arms) yields the same `economic_state_t` as the live sequencer, including `balances_t`, `escrows_t`, `stakes_t`, and `task_markets_t` (with `total_escrow` re-derived correctly). A property test over 1000 random valid sequences confirms `assert_total_ctf_conserved(genesis, q_after, &[])` AND `task_market.total_escrow == Σ escrows[e].amount where e.task_id matches` hold at every step. (Tests I29 + I30.)

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-16.x.fix_charter_2026-05-04.md:84:4. `generate_markov_capsule_does_not_write_global_latest` — invoke `generate_markov_capsule` (or its library entry-point if the binary cannot be invoked from a unit test) with `--out-dir <tmp>`; assert `tmp/LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` does NOT exist; assert `tmp/MARKOV_TB-*.json` exists; assert `handover/markov_capsules/LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` still does not exist.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-16.x.fix_charter_2026-05-04.md:90:- `load_tape` body: replace `if inputs.markov_pointer.exists() { … } else { None }` with `match &inputs.markov_pointer { Some(p) if p.exists() => Some(read_markov_capsule(p, &cas)?), Some(p) => return Err(AuditError::MarkovPointerUnresolvable(format!("{:?}", p))), None => None }`. **Refinement**: per architect Q2.c, the CLI must distinguish "genesis" from "passed-but-not-found"; absent CLI flag → `None`; present-but-FS-absent → BLOCK (not silent None).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-16.x.fix_charter_2026-05-04.md:101:**`--prior-chain-runtime-repo` resolver semantic**: the prior chain's tip Markov capsule cid must be discoverable from the prior chain's `runtime_repo` itself (in-tape). For α immediate, the resolver is permitted to be a thin wrapper that reads a per-run `MARKOV_TB-*.json` from a known per-run subdir (NOT the global pointer). The full Art. 0.4 path B in-tape encoding lands in TB-16.x.2.4.

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:360:No new `RejectionClass` variants in `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs`. (TB-3 added `InsufficientBalance` for richness; TB-4 reuses it.) The five new TransitionError → PolicyViolation mappings are intentionally clustered under the existing class because the L4ERejectionClass taxonomy is coarser than TransitionError taxonomy (5 vs 22+ variants); P4 Information Loom reads the finer-grained TransitionError variant from L4.E payload `details` field, not the L4ERejectionClass tag alone. **Open Atom-1 design question**: confirm the L4.E `details` payload format already carries TransitionError variant name — or this clustering loses signal. (TB-3 audit closed this for TB-3's variants; TB-4 verifies same for new variants.)
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:368:- **`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`** — `TxKind` enum already has `Verify` and `Challenge` variants (TB-2 / TB-3 inherit); no extension. apply_one match arms gain accepted-spine paths (currently only WorkTx + TaskOpen + EscrowLock have accepted paths; Verify + Challenge are stub-NotYetImplemented routes that go to L4.E).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:369:- **`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs`** — UNTOUCHED (no new RejectionClass variants).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:427:2. **No use of `economy::ledger::AcceptedLedger` as the production accepted L4** (TB-2 #6 inherited).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:540:> Reconstructing `QState` from canonical L4 alone (extending TB-3's `replay_full_transition` with the 2 new accepted-spine arms) yields the same `economic_state_t` as the live sequencer, including `balances_t`, `stakes_t` (now multi-role: YES + verifier-bond), `challenge_cases_t` (NEW: bond + opened_at_round + target_work_tx). A property test over 1000 random valid sequences of `TaskOpen + EscrowLock + Work + Verify + Challenge` confirms `assert_total_ctf_conserved(genesis, q_after, &[])` AND `task_market.total_escrow == Σ escrows[e].amount` AND **`challenge_cases_t[c].opened_at_round == q.q_t.current_round at accept time`** hold at every step. The window-anchor pinpoint test asserts the exact equality `opened_at_round == sequencer.q.q_t.current_round_at_dispatch_for(challenge_tx_id)`. The no-drift CI test asserts no `NoStakeTx` / `VerifierBondTx` / `ChallengeStakeTx` / `VerifierStakeTx` literal exists in src/ (per directive § 5.1). (Tests I41 + I42 + I43 + I44.)
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:609:- WP architecture § 1.3 (typed schemas) + § 5 Layer 4 (transition ledger): `handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_v4_WHITEPAPER_v2_2026-04-27_ANTI_OREO_RESTORATION.md`

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:30:- **P1:5** — accepted tx changes `state_root_t` / `ledger_root_t` (re-discharged through production binary, not just `cargo test`).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:34:- **P1:9** — rejected raw diagnostics do NOT enter materialized read view (re-discharged on the production ledger writer's `RejectedSubmissionRecord` serde shield).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:35:- **P2:6** — all Agent outputs enter CAS; ledger records CID (first time discharged at all in production binary).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:68:Per architect ruling D5: the prior `handover/evidence/tb_{1..5}_smoke_*` directories are **smoke evidence** — paper trail, not chain. From TB-6 onward, when (and only when) an evidence directory contains a real on-disk ledger chain produced by a production binary, it may be called a **ChainTape smoke** / **smoke tape**. This charter holds itself to the new naming.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:107:| `src/bin/verify_chaintape.rs` (new) OR `src/bottom_white/ledger/verify_cli.rs` | Atom 4 — CLI entry for replay verifier | additive |

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:124:- **Atom 1** (production runtime repo bootstrap) — 2-3 in-crate unit tests confirming `TuringBus` with chaintape config produces a non-`None` sequencer + non-`None` ledger writer; an idempotent bootstrap test (re-running on existing repo doesn't corrupt).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:146:  ledger_entries.jsonl         (exported view; canonical source = .git refs)
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:184:10. **No `q_state.rs` schema mutation** beyond additive serde-default fields strictly required by Atom 5 (and only if a field is required; preference is to keep audit-trail records out of Q entirely and store them at the ledger / CAS layer).
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:242:> An LLM-driven evaluator run on `mathd_algebra_107` (or equivalent simple problem) produces a `runtime_repo/` containing a real on-disk ledger chain. The chain has at least one `LedgerEntry` with valid `parent_ledger_root` + `resulting_ledger_root` + `tx_payload_cid` + `system_signature`. Tampering with any byte of any entry detectably breaks chain verification. (Atom 4 `verify_chaintape` exits 0 on the unmodified chain; non-zero on tampered chain.)
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:319:- **Q1**: `TuringBus::new_legacy()` extension vs. parallel `TuringBus::new_with_chaintape()` constructor? (Preference: new constructor; legacy stays for tests that don't need a real ledger writer.)

./handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:10:# 2026-04-30 TB-2 charter (active) — "P1/P3 Runtime Boundary Closure + RSP-1". Goal: real WorkTx traverses Sequencer::dispatch_transition; accepted → canonical L4 (transition_ledger + LedgerWriter, NOT economy::ledger::AcceptedLedger which stays a TB-1 primitive); rejected → L4.E with submit_id; RSP-1 admission via WorkTx.stake>0 + seeded EconomicState escrow. STEP_B-protected target: src/state/sequencer.rs. Forbidden Day-1: ledger I/O inside dispatch_transition; new TypedTx variants; non-empty exempt_tx_kinds at runtime; P5/P6/h_vppu work; AcceptedLedger as production accepted spine. Charter: handover/tracer_bullets/TB-2_charter_2026-04-30.md. STEP_B preflight: handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:14:# 2026-05-01 TB-6 ACTIVE — "P2 Agent Runtime: Production ChainTape Wire-up". Architect ruling 2026-05-01 (`handover/directives/2026-05-01_TB6_ARCHITECT_RULING.md`) selected Path A (P2 Agent Runtime / Production ChainTape Wire-up) over Path B (RSP-3.2 Slash) per D1. 7 binding decisions ruled: D1 Path A; D2 chain-backed smoke = HARD requirement from TB-6; D3 hybrid-by-risk audit (TB-6 = production wire-up class → Codex impl + Gemini arch with degraded fallback); D4 `cargo test --workspace` canonical with mandated reporting shape; D5 historical "smoke tape" → "smoke evidence" rename (tape only for ledger chain); D6 5 memory updates approved; D7 NO constitution amendment (this is roadmap/testing-platform gap, not constitutional). Goal: at least one LLM-driven run traverses rtool/context → Agent proposal → WorkTx → Sequencer::apply_one → LedgerWriter::commit → on-disk ChainTape → replay/verify_chain/reconstruct, producing ≥1 accepted L4 + ≥1 rejected L4.E entries with valid system_signature + parent_ledger_root chain + CAS payload linkage + agent audit trail (proposal CIDs only; NO chain-of-thought). 8 atoms per ruling § 3.6: Atom 0 charter+naming cleanup → Atom 1 production runtime repo bootstrap (RuntimeChaintapeConfig; bus.rs + main.rs; STEP_B preflight) → Atom 2 evaluator → Sequencer adapter → Atom 3 chain-backed smoke run (mathd_algebra_107) → Atom 4 verify_chaintape CLI → Atom 5 agent audit trail → Atom 6 fork visibility → Atom 7 audit + ship. Forbidden: SlashTx, NodeMarket/RSP-M, AMM, P6 expansion, MetaTape, public chain, new TypedTx variants, new state-root domains, new monetary cascade, agent chain-of-thought broadcast/persistence, calling pre-TB-6 paper trail "smoke tape"/"chaintape"/"tape", Gemini-degraded-mode label-omission. Charter: handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md. STEP_B preflight (deferred to Atom 1): handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md.
./handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:15:# 2026-05-01 TB-7 ACTIVE — "P2 Frame B: Per-LLM-Proposal WorkTx Routing". Architect ruling 2026-05-01 (`handover/directives/2026-05-01_TB7_ARCHITECT_RULING.md`) re-classifies TB-6 as Frame A only (Frame B: RED) and authorizes TB-7 as Frame B = per-LLM-proposal WorkTx routing through bus.submit_typed_tx as **AUTHORITATIVE** path (NOT "also emit"). 5 binding decisions ruled: D1 Option A (Frame B; legacy bus.append must be removed / ChainTape-projected / shadow_only-annotated — "also emit" forbidden); D2 run-local Ed25519 keypair with `run-local identity, not durable reputation identity` caveat; D3 narrowed OMEGA scope (WorkTx + VerifyTx; ChallengeWindow OPEN; no FinalizeRewardTx; no SlashTx); D4 ChainDerivedRunFacts (renamed from chain-derived PPUT; bit-exact on §4.4 structural field set: solved/verified/tx_count/proposal_count/golden_path_token_count/gp_payload/gp_path/gp_proof_file/tactic_diversity/tool_dist/failed_branch_count; time-sensitive fields excluded); D5 production wire-up Class 2 audit (Codex impl + Gemini arch with degraded fallback) + new ProposalTelemetry CAS atom (Atom 1.5). 7 ship gates: Gate 1 authoritative path; Gate 2 chain_proposal_count==evaluator_proposal_count; Gate 3 ≥1 L4 + ≥1 L4.E (forced rejection allowed only with forced_rejection_for_gate_3=true label); Gate 4 agent + system signature verification; Gate 5 ProposalTelemetry CAS retrievability per WorkTx.proposal_cid; Gate 6 chain-derived run facts == evaluator structural facts; Gate 7 legacy bypass regression test (repo-wide grep + shadow_only annotation enforced). Goal: every meaningful LLM proposal -> WorkTx/VerifyTx -> bus.submit_typed_tx (authoritative) -> Sequencer::apply_one -> L4 or L4.E -> CAS-linked ProposalTelemetry -> replay-verifiable ChainDerivedRunFacts. 8 atoms: Atom 0 charter ratification + ARCHITECT_RULING archive + memory updates -> Atom 1 src/runtime/agent_keypairs.rs + agent_pubkeys.json -> Atom 1.5 src/runtime/proposal_telemetry.rs (NEW per D5) -> Atom 2 evaluator append-branch authoritative routing -> Atom 3 evaluator OMEGA-branch routing -> Atom 4 verify_chaintape extension (agent-sig + ProposalTelemetry CAS lookup) -> Atom 5 src/runtime/chain_derived_run_facts.rs (renamed per D4) -> Atom 6 chain-backed real-LLM smoke on mathd_algebra_107 -> Atom 7 audit + ship. Forbidden additions #21-30 inherited; +#31 legacy bus.append as authoritative state mutation; +#32 calling Atom 5 output "chain-derived PPUT"; +#33 forced rejection without label. Post-TB-7 sequencing override (charter §13; supersedes TB-6 ruling §4.5): TB-8 audit dashboard -> TB-9 minimal payout (FinalizeRewardTx single-solver/single-verifier/no-royalty/no-DAG/no-NodeMarket) -> TB-10 beta launch (narrow Lean problem set, real ChainTape + replay + escrow/payout) -> TB-11 NodeMarket v0 (FirstLong/Short positions + PriceIndex v0; NOT tradable) -> TB-12+ Polymarket-like full market. NodeMarket trading / AMM / public chain / MetaTape / multi-org / full RSP-4 settlement / royalty / long-term reputation / P6 PPUT research expansion / h_vppu polish DEFERRED post-MVP per ruling §6. 2 new memory rules: feedback_risk_class_audit (Class 0–4 audit standard) + feedback_launch_priority (MVP-vertical-slice over NodeMarket). Charter: handover/tracer_bullets/TB-7_charter_2026-05-01.md (renamed from _draft_ at ratification).

./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:151:## §6. Three-FC alignment statement (FC1 + FC2 + FC3 per CLAUDE.md §3)
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:153:Per CLAUDE.md §3 every non-trivial TB must declare which flowchart gates it touches. A4 touches admission semantics (FC1) and the agent_verifications_t state index (FC2 boot-from-genesis replayability; FC3 capsule derivation discipline). All three FCs verified at HEAD `69910fe` against the 6-cell smoke `stage_b3_smoke_a4_20260510T222030Z`.
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:159:- **`verify_peer` dispatch is a separate code path** from `step` / `complete` / `append`. It does NOT call the oracle (no Lean verification) and does NOT call `r2_write_attempt_telemetry` (no AttemptTelemetry CAS write). It only submits a VerifyTx via `bus.submit_typed_tx`. The submitted VerifyTx routes to L4 (accept) or L4.E (reject) per sequencer admission — both are already counted in `l4_work_attempt_count` / `l4e_work_attempt_count` (note: the "work" naming is legacy; the chain accepts both WorkTx and VerifyTx onto the L4 ledger; the FC1 counters cover both per CLAUDE.md §6 OBS_TB18R_INV1_NONLLM_TX clarification).
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:164:### §6.2 FC2 — Boot/Genesis Gate (replayable from genesis + tape + CAS)
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:169:- **`agent_verifications_t` is derived state**: post-accepted-VerifyTx, the sequencer inserts `(verifier_agent, target_work_tx)` into `agent_verifications_t` at Step-5b. Replay from genesis through L4 ledger reconstructs the same set deterministically (each accepted VerifyTx contributes one pair; ChallengeTx and other tx kinds do not touch this index).
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:174:### §6.3 FC3 — Meta/Markov Gate (no global latest pointer; capsule derived from tape+CAS; raw logs shielded)
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:179:- no global latest pointer (`LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` forbidden)
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:185:- **No global latest pointer**: A4 does not write `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` or any similar global-state file. Empirical verification: **6/6 cells** have no `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt`.
./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:195:| FC2 runtime_repo/ present | per-cell dir presence | **6/6** ✓ |

./handover/directives/2026-04-29_9_phase_roadmap.md:24:> TuringOS 的路线不是从宏大叙事开始，而是从一个本地 GitTape 开始：两个 Agent、一个只读宪法、一个 CAS、一个 JSONL ledger、几个确定性谓词、一个 wtool、一个 escrow 表。只要这个最小系统能证明黑盒不能直接写状态、错误不会污染上下文、奖金不能绕过验证发放，TuringOS 的第一块秩序晶体就已经形成。

./handover/directives/2026-05-06_TB18R_PHASE_2_REMEDIATION_DIRECTIVE.md:195:**Sequencer impact (Class-4 admission)**: the sequencer's `From<LeanErrorClass> for RejectionClass` mapping (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs`, R3-shipped) is **not** affected — sequencer only consumes `AttemptOutcome` for the rejection arm via `outcome` discriminator, and `step_partial_ok` does not enter the rejection arm (predicate_passes=false routes to L4.E only when AttemptOutcome ∈ {LeanFail, ParseFail, SorryBlock, LlmErr}). `PartialAccepted` is a **non-rejection, non-acceptance third state** — `step_partial_ok` continues to be CAS-only per R3 §1.3 (no L4 / no L4.E entry). The sequencer mapping function gains a single guard: if `outcome == PartialAccepted` reaches the rejection arm (which it must not), panic in test-build / log-warn-and-fall-back-to-PredicateFailed in release-build.

./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:142:是一个单行 hex pointer，用于 `audit_tape --markov-pointer` 解析本次 audit 应该验证哪一个 Markov capsule；它当前指向的 capsule 字节只存在于某一次 TB-16 R3 Round 2 `P8_completeset_b` 的 per-problem CAS 中，不在 global/project-root CAS，也不在其他 per-problem CAS 中。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:155:TB-16.x.1 把 `.ok()` collapse 改成 fail-closed BLOCK 是正确的，因为它让隐藏问题暴露出来；材料也明确说 pre-fix 的 `.ok()` collapse 曾经把 pointer-CAS mismatch 静默掩盖成 `Skipped`，导致 CR-15.6 "Markov default prevents context poisoning" 被绕过。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:161:但 TB-16 不能在 global Markov pointer 仍被当作 canonical reference 的情况下 ship。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:173:1. 它是 filesystem-side global。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:198:#### §B.2.Q2.a Markov capsule 是不是 FC1 / FC2 / FC3 的 flowchart node？
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:202:Markov capsule 是派生视图 / evidence compression，不是三张 flowchart 的原生节点。材料明确说明，Markov capsule 由 TB-15 引入，其注释称它是 evidence compression，不是 hidden source of truth；每个字段应能从 chain + CAS 派生，因此它落在 Art. 0.2 的"派生视图 + 守恒义务"范畴，而不是 flowchart node。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:264:#### §B.2.Q3 未来多任务 continuation 的 Markov inheritance 规则是什么？
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:274:Markov inheritance 结构性编码在 tape_t 中。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:277:这是长期正确方向，也就是材料里的 Option β。它说在 Art. 0.4 path B 下，chain continuation 意味着每个新任务的 runtime_repo 扩展前一个任务的 git history，不是 separate repos；此时前一个 task 的 Markov capsule 在同一个 CAS 中，不需要全局 pointer。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:289:Markov capsule 不是 flowchart node；
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:291:修复方式是删除 global pointer 或把继承锚定到 tape continuation；
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:292:不修改 FC1/FC2/FC3。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:309:6. fresh chain 默认 no inherited Markov。
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:336:per-run Markov JSON 放在 handover/evidence/tb_*/markov/
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:346:markov_pointer_no_global_parallel_ledger
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:349:generate_markov_capsule_does_not_write_global_latest
./handover/directives/2026-05-04_TB16_OBS_R022_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:380:  delete/de-canonicalize global pointer

./handover/directives/2026-05-01_TB6_ARCHITECT_FULL_PROMPT.md:15:TuringOS v4 has shipped 5 tracer-bullets (TB-1..TB-5) over 2026-04-29 to 2026-04-30, each adding kernel functionality to `src/state/sequencer.rs` + `src/state/typed_tx.rs` + `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`. The cumulative claim is "TuringOS runtime kernel structurally enforces L4 / L4.E split + Anti-Oreo agent-vs-system separation + RSP-1/2/3.0/3.1 economy core."
./handover/directives/2026-05-01_TB6_ARCHITECT_FULL_PROMPT.md:22:- **The "smoke tape" in `handover/evidence/tb_N_smoke_*/` directories is paper trail** — `*_run.log` (stdout dumps) + `proof_n1.lean` (source) + `README.md` (narrative). None traverse `Sequencer::apply_one` → `LedgerWriter::commit`. No `system_signature`, no `parent_ledger_root` chain, no replay path against this evidence. The naming is a v3 PaperTape-era metaphor.
./handover/directives/2026-05-01_TB6_ARCHITECT_FULL_PROMPT.md:63:| `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` | `LedgerEntry` (with `parent_ledger_root` chain + `system_signature`) + `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` + `InMemoryLedgerWriter` + `Git2LedgerWriter` (line 642; on-disk persistence; **never instantiated in production**) + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry` | TB-1/2 |

./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:49:`src/runtime/audit_assertions.rs:2580-2656`. **TRACE_MATRIX annotation (line :2580)**: `FC2-N34`.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:85:This confirms FC1-N31..N40 covers the runtime/proposal loop (where partial-verdict arises). FC2 covers the audit layer (where assert_45 lives).
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:91:### §1.1 Q1 — Does PartialVerdict belong to FC1 (proposal flow) or FC2 (audit flow)?
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:101:assert_45 (FC2-N34) is the **audit-time invariant over an FC1 object**. FC2 reads FC1's witness; it does not own the witness's semantics.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:103:**Implication**: any fix that "improves" assert_45 to admit partial-verdict is treating a symptom on FC2 while the constitutional contract sits on FC1. The contract that says "what shape may a `LeanResult` take?" is an FC1 contract.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:107:**Answer: Primarily predicate evidence (FC1). Secondarily a read-only witness for FC2 audit walks.**
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:112:- **Audit witness (FC2 role)**: `LeanResult` CAS objects are walked by assert_45 + assert_44 + (future) attempt_chain_root computation. FC2 reads them; FC2 does **not** mint or modify them.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:114:The asymmetry matters: FC2 invariants are downstream of FC1 schema. If FC1 schema admits an ambiguous state (e.g., `error_class=None` could mean partial-verdict OR a hypothetical bug), FC2 invariants cannot fully discriminate it.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:116:**Implication**: the right place to typify "partial-verdict is a legitimate third state" is the **FC1 schema**, not the FC2 invariant.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:136:### §1.4 Q4 — Does R8 change FC2 audit invariant or FC1 proposal semantics?
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:138:**Answer: R8 as currently shipped changes only the FC2 audit invariant. The FC1 proposal semantics remain unchanged.**
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:141:- `src/runtime/audit_assertions.rs:2580-2656` (FC2-N34) — replaced iff with three implications. **FC2 only.**
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:148:So R8 is an **FC2-only patch over an FC1 ambiguity**. It is the constitutionally minimal way to clear the round-1 Q13 VETO without a Class-4 schema bump — but it leaves the FC1 ambiguity in place.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:150:**This is the workaround**: the FC1 contract still has three semantic states multiplexed onto one untyped triple; FC2 just stopped checking the multiplexing.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:172:- **Why not plain β**: as §1.3 establishes, `(0, false, None)` is a multiplexed triple; relaxing FC2 to admit it loses defect-detection on FC1.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:173:- **Why β-with-typing**: introduces a typed third state on FC1 (where it constitutionally belongs per §1.1), then makes FC2 invariant typed-aware. This is the constitutionally-clean form.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:251:4. **Allows `verified` to remain the FC2 ship-gate metric** while `verdict_kind` becomes the typed discriminator. Existing `pput_verified` and ChainDerivedRunFacts code paths that read `verified: bool` continue to work without retrofit.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:276:R8-as-shipped is **interim**: the FC2 invariant relaxation is correct *as a temporary fix to the round-1 Q13 VETO*, conditional on Phase 2 typed-PartialAccepted landing. Without Phase 2, R8 leaves a semantic hole.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:296:**Conclusion**: assert_45 is FC2-only, with no FC3 cross-edge. R8's invariant relaxation does not perturb economic flow. This validates that R8 is FC2-scope, and that Phase 2 typed-PartialAccepted will likewise be FC1-FC2-only without economic-engine downstream.
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:313:| Q1 (PartialVerdict in FC1 or FC2?) | **FC1** (LeanResult is FC1-N41; state born in evaluator proposal loop) |
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:316:| Q4 (R8 changes FC2 invariant or FC1 semantics?) | **R8 changes FC2 invariant only** — FC1 semantics still ambiguous |
./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:317:| FC3 cross-edge? | **None** — assert_45 is FC2-scope; no economic downstream |
