./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:3:> **Status**: DRAFT. G1.1 (resume-mode genesis branch) is the load-bearing
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:4:> Class-4 STEP_B atom of G-Phase per architect's binding G1-before-G4
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:13:- **TB**: TB-G atom **G1.1** (RuntimeChaintapeConfig resume mode)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:14:- **Charter**: `handover/tracer_bullets/TB_G_GENERATIVE_ARENA_charter_2026-05-11.md` §1 Module G1
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:17:- **Class**: **4 STEP_B** — touches `src/state/sequencer.rs` (new constructor) + `src/runtime/mod.rs` (admission of non-empty repo at boot) + indirectly the FC2-Boot semantic boundary
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:18:- **STEP_B branch (forward)**: `feat/g1-1-resume-mode` (parallel branch per CLAUDE.md §12; NOT yet cut)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:21:- **FC-trace**: FC2-Boot **extension** — replay-from-genesis semantic preserved; introduces a new admission path (`build_chaintape_sequencer` accepts non-empty repo iff env-gated `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME=1` AND `reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` succeeds). FC1 untouched. FC3 untouched. **Constitutional anchor**: G-009 HEAD_t Path C / Stage A3 multi-ref ChainTape — `reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` already reconstructs `QState` from `refs/chaintape/{l4,l4e,cas}` (SG-A3.4 GREEN); G1.1 turns that capability into a sequencer-construction boundary.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:23:## §1. What G1.1 closes
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:38:That "future TB" is G1.1. G1.1 lifts the fail-closed by adding **resume mode** as an explicit, env-gated admission path. Critically:
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:42:- **`Sequencer.next_logical_t` correctness**: resume branch sets `next_logical_t = chain_length` so the strict `len + 1` invariant in `Git2LedgerWriter` holds on the next commit. Architect SG-G1.2 verbatim.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:43:- **`QState` reconstruction**: resume branch calls `reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` (existing helper at `src/state/head_t_witness.rs::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs`, GREEN under Stage A3 R3.5 + R5 smoke). No new replay logic.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:44:- **Pinned-pubkey continuity**: resume branch reads existing `pinned_pubkeys.json` if present (epoch preserved across resume); else generates fresh per CLAUDE.md FC2 system-pubkey discipline.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:46:## §2. Substrate change summary (forward — to land on branch `feat/g1-1-resume-mode`)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:54:| `src/runtime/mod.rs` | +`RuntimeChaintapeConfig.resume_existing_chain: bool` (env-read `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME==1` in `from_env`; default `false`) + `build_chaintape_sequencer` branch on `config.resume_existing_chain`: if `true` AND `Git2LedgerWriter::open` finds non-empty `refs/transitions/main`, call `reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` to rebuild `QState` + open existing `CasStore` + read existing `pinned_pubkeys.json` if present + call `Sequencer::new_at_logical_t(initial_q, len)`. **Strict invariant**: if resume succeeds, `next_logical_t == chain_length` and the next commit's `parent_state_root` matches the chain head's `state_root`. |
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:61:| `src/state/head_t_witness.rs` | (REUSE ONLY; no edit expected) `reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` already exists (Stage A3) and produces the `(QState, chain_length, l4e_count, cas_count)` tuple consumed by the resume branch. |
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:67:| `tests/constitution_g1_resume.rs` (NEW) | 5+ tests covering SG-G1.1..G1.5: <br>• `resume_on_empty_repo_equals_legacy_genesis` — `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME=1` on a fresh empty path produces byte-equal `QState` to legacy `build_chaintape_sequencer` (default branch).<br>• `resume_on_n_entry_chain_sets_next_logical_t_to_n` — bootstrap fresh → commit N entries → drop sequencer → re-`build_chaintape_sequencer` with resume=1 → assert `Sequencer.next_logical_t == N` AND next commit's `parent_state_root` matches.<br>• `resume_balances_reconstruction_matches_forward_replay` — fresh forward run of K accepted `WorkTx` produces `balances_t_A`; same chain replayed via resume produces `balances_t_B`; assert byte-equal.<br>• `non_empty_runtime_repo_only_fires_when_resume_false` — non-empty repo + resume=0 still returns `BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo` (back-compat regression gate).<br>• `pinned_pubkeys_preserved_across_resume` — fresh bootstrap writes `pinned_pubkeys.json`; resume reads it and `epoch` carries forward (no re-pin). |
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:73:| `handover/alignment/TRACE_FLOWCHART_MATRIX.md` | +FC2-Boot row: "Resume from existing `refs/transitions/main` (env-gated)" with `tests/constitution_g1_resume.rs` binding. |
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:78:- `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` — no new signing domain.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:79:- `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` — no new TxKind id; `Git2LedgerWriter::open` already supports opening non-empty repos (the fail-closed gate lives in `build_chaintape_sequencer`, not in `Git2LedgerWriter`).
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:80:- `genesis_payload.toml` — no rehash. Resume does NOT mutate genesis; it consumes the existing genesis_report.json from the resumed repo.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:84:## §3. Ship gates (SG-G1.1..SG-G1.5 per charter §1 Module G1)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:88:| SG-G1.1 | Resume on empty repo == legacy bootstrap (byte-equal genesis) | `resume_on_empty_repo_equals_legacy_genesis` | RED until implementation |

./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:100:G1.1 touches sequencer admission (new constructor) AND introduces a new boot-time admission path (non-empty repo allowed under env gate). **Both qualify as Class 4 surfaces.** Per `feedback_no_batch_class4_signoff`, G1.1 cannot be batched with G3.2 or G4.2; each gets its own §8 packet + sign-off.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:104:- **Tape-first**: resume reads from `refs/chaintape/{l4,l4e,cas}` — the canonical tape. No memory-only state. (CLAUDE.md §2.2)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:105:- **No ghost liquidity**: resume does not mint. (CLAUDE.md §13)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:106:- **No price-as-truth**: resume does not touch price. (CLAUDE.md §13)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:107:- **Dashboard materialized view only**: resume does not touch dashboard. (CLAUDE.md §5.4)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:108:- **No real funds**: resume is local FS. (CLAUDE.md §20 freeze list)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:109:- **No public chain**: resume is local FS. (CLAUDE.md §20 freeze list)
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:110:- **FC2-Boot replay determinism**: resume IS replay. Architect §4.1 G-009 HEAD_t Path C requires "every accepted transition updates HEAD_t" + "replay reconstructs HEAD_t" — resume is the **boot-time** instance of that replay. Existing Stage A3 SG-A3.4 (replay byte-equality) covers the replay correctness; G1.1 SG-G1.3 layers a balances-byte-equal check on top.
./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:111:- **No global Markov pointer**: resume reads from refs, not from a global pointer. (CLAUDE.md §3.3)

./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:128:   - **Q9**: resume branch is mentioned in `TRACE_FLOWCHART_MATRIX.md` FC2-Boot row.

./handover/post-mortems/ROOT_CAUSE_TB18_DELAY_2026-05-06.md:97:A TB whose actual work-class set is broader than its charter title produces a **misclassified gate**: the audit gate at G1/G2 expects benchmark-class evidence (numbers, batch summaries, ladder progression), not architecture-class evidence (invariant proofs, schema bumps, tape-granularity tests). When the audit reveals architecture defects, the audit's own framing is mismatched.
./handover/post-mortems/ROOT_CAUSE_TB18_DELAY_2026-05-06.md:113:This is the equation R4 eventually shipped (G1-ratified) and that R8/R9 evidence finally validated 6/6 empirical PASS.
./handover/post-mortems/ROOT_CAUSE_TB18_DELAY_2026-05-06.md:123:P49 ran 32 externalized LLM-Lean cycles; only 1 ChainTape WorkTx was emitted. The chain compressed authoritative ledger state by 31×. This is the concrete defect that produced the VETO.

   755	
   756	/// TB-G G1.1: decode a 64-char hex string into a 32-byte ed25519 pubkey.
   757	/// Used only by `bootstrap_resume_state`; kept private. Mirrors the
   758	/// `verify.rs::decode_pubkey_hex` decode path but yields the array
   759	/// shape directly (no intermediate `Vec`).
   760	fn decode_pubkey_hex_32(hex: &str) -> Result<[u8; 32], BootstrapError> {
   761	    if hex.len() != 64 {
   762	        return Err(BootstrapError::Keypair(format!(
   763	            "pubkey_hex expected 64 chars, got {}",
   764	            hex.len()
   765	        )));
   766	    }
   767	    let mut out = [0u8; 32];
   768	    for i in 0..32 {
   769	        let byte = u8::from_str_radix(&hex[i * 2..i * 2 + 2], 16).map_err(|e| {
   770	            BootstrapError::Keypair(format!("pubkey_hex decode at byte {i}: {e}"))
   771	        })?;
   772	        out[i] = byte;
   773	    }
   774	    Ok(out)
   775	}
   776	
   777	// ── Driver wrapper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   778	
   779	/// Runtime-side driver loop. NOT `Sequencer::run`: see module doc-comment.
   780	///
   781	/// Invariants:
   782	/// - On `shutdown_rx` signal: closes `queue_rx` (refuses new sends), drains
   783	///   the remaining queue synchronously via `Sequencer::apply_one`, returns.
   784	/// - On `queue_rx.recv() == None` (all senders dropped): returns.
   785	/// - `tokio::select! { biased; ... }` ensures the shutdown signal wins races
   786	///   against pending `recv()` calls (otherwise busy queues could starve shutdown).
   787	async fn run_chaintape_driver(
   788	    sequencer: Arc<Sequencer>,
   789	    mut queue_rx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<SubmissionEnvelope>,
   790	    mut shutdown_rx: oneshot::Receiver<()>,
   791	) {
   792	    loop {
   793	        tokio::select! {
   794	            biased;
   795	            _ = &mut shutdown_rx => {
   796	                // Refuse new sends, then drain remaining envelopes.
   797	                queue_rx.close();
   798	                while let Some(envelope) = queue_rx.recv().await {
   799	                    if let Err(e) = sequencer.apply_one(envelope) {
   800	                        log::debug!("chaintape driver drain apply_one rejected: {e}");
   801	                    }
   802	                }
   803	                return;
   804	            }
   805	            env = queue_rx.recv() => {
   806	                match env {
   807	                    Some(envelope) => {
   808	                        if let Err(e) = sequencer.apply_one(envelope) {
   809	                            log::debug!("chaintape driver apply_one rejected: {e}");
   810	                        }
   811	                    }
   812	                    None => return,
   813	                }
   814	            }
   815	        }
   816	    }
   817	}
   818	
   819	// ── Tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   820	//

102:### FC2 invariant battery (TB-C0 NEW gate tests)
103-
104-| Invariant | Test | What it asserts |
105-|---|---|---|
106:| **FC2-INV1** genesis exists | `fc2_genesis_report_exists` | `genesis_payload.toml` parses + trust root verifies |
107:| **FC2-INV2** init-only mint | `fc2_on_init_only_mint` + `fc2_no_post_init_mint` | total Coin supply set at on_init; never mints elsewhere |
108:| **FC2-INV3** no memory preseed | `fc2_no_memory_only_preseed` | code-grep: no `q.economic_state_t.insert(` outside on_init helpers |
109:| **FC2-INV4** chain events | `fc2_taskopen_escrowlock_are_chain_events` | TaskOpen/EscrowLock issued via Sequencer dispatch, not memory mutation |
110:| **FC2-INV5** replayable | `fc2_run_replayable_from_genesis_tape_cas` | tear down state, replay from genesis_report + L4 + CAS, recover identical chain_derived_run_facts |
111:| **FC2-INV6** pubkeys verify | `fc2_system_pubkeys_verify` | system tx signature verifies under genesis_payload.toml-pinned pubkey |
112:| **FC2-INV7** registry resolves | `fc2_agent_registry_resolves` | agent_pubkeys.json → AgentKeypairRegistry resolves correct pubkey |
113:| **FC2-INV8** resume-from-existing-chain (TB-G G1.1; architect §8 SIGNED 2026-05-11) | `tests/constitution_g1_resume.rs` (sg_g1_1 .. sg_g1_5) | env-gated `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME=1` admits non-empty `refs/transitions/main` and rebuilds QState via the canonical FC2 Boot replay primitive `replay_full_transition` (same primitive `verify_chaintape` uses); resume preserves `pinned_pubkeys.json` (epoch continuity) and seeds `Sequencer.next_logical_t = chain_length` so the strict `len + 1` invariant holds on the next commit; default `resume_existing_chain = false` preserves the original TB-6 `NonEmptyRuntimeRepo` fail-closed gate (SG-G1.4 back-compat) |

---
name: verification-before-completion
description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
---

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ≠ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ≠ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ≠ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
✅ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
❌ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
✅ Write → Run (pass) → Revert fix → Run (MUST FAIL) → Restore → Run (pass)
❌ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
✅ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
❌ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
✅ Re-read plan → Create checklist → Verify each → Report gaps or completion
❌ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
✅ Agent reports success → Check VCS diff → Verify changes → Report actual state
❌ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion → redirect → rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.

   731	pub struct Git2LedgerWriter {

   744	// ── Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 multi-ref ChainTape (additive) ─────────────────────

   749	// The C1 baseline's `refs/transitions/main` is preserved as a backward-compat
   750	// alias; every accepted-transition commit dual-writes to `refs/chaintape/l4`
   751	// so a fresh checkout can reconstruct HEAD_t via either ref family. The L4.E

   765	impl Git2LedgerWriter {
   766	    /// Open or initialize a `Git2LedgerWriter` rooted at `repo_path`.
   767	    /// Creates the underlying git repo if it doesn't exist; resolves the
   768	    /// existing `refs/transitions/main` if present and seeds `head_oid` + `len`.
   769	    pub fn open(repo_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, LedgerWriterError> {
   770	        let repo_path = repo_path.to_path_buf();
   771	        let repo = match Repository::open(&repo_path) {
   772	            Ok(r) => r,
   773	            Err(_) => Repository::init(&repo_path).map_err(|e| {
   774	                LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo init: {e}"))
   775	            })?,
   776	        };
   777	
   778	        // Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 R7 (Codex R1 Q1 fix): C2 ref `refs/chaintape/l4`
   779	        // is canonical; `refs/transitions/main` is a backward-compat alias.
   780	        // Prefer C2 if present; fall back to C1 alias if only C1 exists (e.g.
   781	        // pre-A3 evidence). On open() detect divergence between the two and
   782	        // repair by mirroring C2 → C1 (canonical wins). This closes the partial
   783	        // failure window where commit() updates C2 but C1 alias mirror fails.
   784	        let c2_oid = repo
   785	            .find_reference(CHAINTAPE_L4_REF)
   786	            .ok()
   787	            .and_then(|r| r.target());
   788	        let c1_oid = repo
   789	            .find_reference(TRANSITIONS_REF)
   790	            .ok()
   791	            .and_then(|r| r.target());
   792	
   793	        let canonical_oid = match (c2_oid, c1_oid) {
   794	            (Some(c2), Some(c1)) if c2 != c1 => {
   795	                // Divergence detected: C2 wins per CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6 (C1 is alias).
   796	                // Repair by overwriting C1 to match C2.
   797	                repo.reference(
   798	                    TRANSITIONS_REF,
   799	                    c2,
   800	                    true,
   801	                    "C2/C1 divergence repair: aligning C1 alias to canonical C2",
   802	                )
   803	                .map_err(|e| {
   804	                    LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!(
   805	                        "C1 alias repair: {e}"
   806	                    ))
   807	                })?;
   808	                Some(c2)
   809	            }
   810	            (Some(c2), _) => Some(c2),
   811	            (None, Some(c1)) => Some(c1), // pre-A3 evidence: C1-only path
   812	            (None, None) => None,
   813	        };
   814	
   815	        let (head_oid, len) = match canonical_oid {
   816	            Some(oid) => {
   817	                // Walk parents to count chain length.
   818	                let mut n: u64 = 0;
   819	                let mut cursor = Some(oid);
   820	                while let Some(c) = cursor {
   821	                    n += 1;
   822	                    let commit = repo.find_commit(c).map_err(|e| {
   823	                        LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("walk parent: {e}"))
   824	                    })?;
   825	                    cursor = commit.parent(0).ok().map(|p| p.id());
   826	                }
   827	                (Some(oid), n)
   828	            }
   829	            None => (None, 0),
   830	        };
   831	
   832	        Ok(Self {
   833	            repo_path,
   834	            head_oid,
   835	            len,
   836	        })
   837	    }
   838	
   839	    fn open_repo(&self) -> Result<Repository, LedgerWriterError> {
   840	        Repository::open(&self.repo_path)
   841	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo open: {e}")))
   842	    }

  1038	        // the HEAD_t schema is unchanged; the C2 ref family is now the canonical
  1039	        // pointer, with C1 as a repairable alias.
  1040	        repo.reference(
  1041	            TRANSITIONS_REF,
  1042	            new_oid,
  1043	            true,
  1044	            &format!("transition logical_t={} (C1 alias)", entry.logical_t),
  1045	        )
  1046	        .map_err(|e| {
  1047	            LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!(
  1048	                "C1 alias refs/transitions/main update: {e}"
  1049	            ))
  1050	        })?;
  1051	
  1052	        self.head_oid = Some(new_oid);
  1053	        self.len += 1;
  1054	        Ok(entry.resulting_ledger_root)
  1055	    }
  1056	
  1057	    fn read_at(&self, logical_t: u64) -> Result<LedgerEntry, LedgerWriterError> {
  1058	        let bytes = self.read_canonical_bytes(logical_t)?;
  1059	        canonical_decode::<LedgerEntry>(&bytes).map_err(|e| {
  1060	            LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("canonical_decode at {logical_t}: {e}"))
  1061	        })
  1062	    }
  1063	
  1064	    fn len(&self) -> u64 {
  1065	        self.len
  1066	    }
  1067	}

   452	    /// flushes a single JSONL line per record + fsync. `open_jsonl` reads
   453	    /// existing file on construction + replays into `records`.
   454	    JsonlAppend { path: std::path::PathBuf },
   455	}
   456	
   457	impl RejectionEvidenceWriter {
   458	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — empty writer with `InMemory` backend.
   459	    pub fn new() -> Self {
   460	        Self::default()
   461	    }
   462	
   463	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1 + P1:6 (TB-6 Atom 1.2) — open or create a JSONL-backed writer.
   464	    ///
   465	    /// On open:
   466	    /// - If `path` exists, read each line as a `RejectedSubmissionRecord`,
   467	    ///   append to `records`, and `verify_chain()` over the loaded records to
   468	    ///   reject tampering at load time.
   469	    /// - If `path` does not exist, create the file (and parent dirs) empty.
   470	    /// - Either way, set backend to `JsonlAppend(path)` so subsequent
   471	    ///   `append_rejected` calls flush to that path.
   472	    ///
   473	    /// Architect § 3.5 "或等价结构": JSONL chain-hash equivalent to git
   474	    /// `refs/rejections/main`. Each line embeds `prev_hash` + `hash` so
   475	    /// tampering with any line breaks the chain at that line.
   476	    pub fn open_jsonl(
   477	        path: std::path::PathBuf,
   478	    ) -> Result<Self, RejectionEvidenceError> {
   479	        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
   480	            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| {
   481	                RejectionEvidenceError::Io(format!("create parent dir for {path:?}: {e}"))
   482	            })?;
   483	        }
   484	        let mut records: Vec<RejectedSubmissionRecord> = Vec::new();
   485	        if path.exists() {
   486	            let contents = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).map_err(|e| {
   487	                RejectionEvidenceError::Io(format!("read {path:?}: {e}"))
   488	            })?;
   489	            for (idx, line) in contents.lines().enumerate() {
   490	                if line.trim().is_empty() {
   491	                    continue;
   492	                }
   493	                let shadow: JsonlRecord =
   494	                    serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| {
   495	                        RejectionEvidenceError::JsonlParse {
   496	                            line: idx,
   497	                            reason: e.to_string(),
   498	                        }
   499	                    })?;
   500	                records.push(shadow.into());
   501	            }
   502	        } else {
   503	            // Create empty file with parent dirs already ensured above.
   504	            std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
   505	                .create(true)
   506	                .append(true)
   507	                .open(&path)
   508	                .map_err(|e| {
   509	                    RejectionEvidenceError::Io(format!("create {path:?}: {e}"))
   510	                })?;
   511	        }
   512	        let writer = Self {
   513	            records,
   514	            backend: Backend::JsonlAppend { path },
   515	        };
   516	        // Validate chain integrity on load — tampering with any record
   517	        // breaks here.
   518	        writer.verify_chain()?;
   519	        Ok(writer)
   520	    }
   521	
   522	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1 (TB-6 Atom 1.2) — convenience: is this writer JSONL-backed?
   523	    pub fn is_jsonl_backed(&self) -> bool {
   524	        matches!(self.backend, Backend::JsonlAppend { .. })
   525	    }
   526	
   527	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — count of recorded rejections.
   528	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
   529	        self.records.len()
   530	    }
   531	
   532	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — empty predicate.

   472	/// CO1.7-impl A4 — full-mode replay (THE I-DETHASH witness).

   474	/// Validates **every** stage spec § 4 + § 6 promises:

   478	/// 4. system_signature verifies via CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning + pinned pubkeys

   484	/// 9. resulting_ledger_root recomputed via append() matches stored
   485	///
   486	/// **v1.1 C-1 closure**: takes a full `genesis: &QState` (was `genesis_state_root`
   487	/// + `genesis_ledger_root` only). Caller provides the complete genesis state
   488	/// so dispatch_transition can read budget / registries / balances / task markets
   489	/// — fabricating `QState::genesis()` was dropping these fields.
   490	///
   491	/// **Stub-state caveat (CO1.7.5 unblocks)**: while `dispatch_transition`
   492	/// returns `NotYetImplemented` for every variant, replay errors at stage 7
   493	/// for any non-empty chain. Conformance tests exercising stages 1-6.5
   494	/// independently are `#[test]`-runnable now; full state_root reconstruction
   495	/// gates on CO1.7.5.
   496	pub fn replay_full_transition(
   497	    genesis: &crate::state::q_state::QState,
   498	    entries: &[LedgerEntry],
   499	    cas: &dyn LedgerCasView,
   500	    pinned_pubkeys: &crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::PinnedSystemPubkeys,
   501	    predicate_registry: &crate::top_white::predicates::registry::PredicateRegistry,
   502	    tool_registry: &crate::bottom_white::tools::registry::ToolRegistry,
   503	) -> Result<crate::state::q_state::QState, ReplayError> {
   504	    use crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::{
   505	        verify_system_signature, CanonicalMessage,
   506	    };
   507	    use crate::state::sequencer::dispatch_transition;
   508	
   509	    let mut q = genesis.clone();
   510	
   511	    for (i, entry) in entries.iter().enumerate() {
   512	        // Stage 1
   513	        let expected_logical_t = (i as u64) + 1;
   514	        if entry.logical_t != expected_logical_t {
   515	            return Err(ReplayError::LogicalTGap {
   516	                at: i,
   517	                expected: expected_logical_t,
   518	                got: entry.logical_t,
   519	            });
   520	        }
   521	        // Stage 2
   522	        if entry.parent_state_root != q.state_root_t {
   523	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentStateMismatch { at: i });
   524	        }
   525	        // Stage 3

   527	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentLedgerMismatch { at: i });
   528	        }

   530	        // Stage 4: system_signature verify (FullTransition mode only).
   531	        let signing_payload = entry.to_signing_payload();
   532	        let signing_digest = signing_payload.canonical_digest();
   533	        let canonical_msg = CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(signing_digest.0);

   535	            &entry.system_signature,
   536	            &canonical_msg,
   537	            entry.epoch,
   538	            pinned_pubkeys,
   539	        ) {
   540	            return Err(ReplayError::BadSignature { at: i });
   541	        }

   549	        // error from CasMissing).

   552	                at: i,
   553	                reason: e.to_string(),
   554	            })?;

   556	        // Stage 6.5 (v1.1 C-3): tx_kind envelope vs decoded payload kind MUST match.
   557	        // Otherwise a signed envelope claiming `Work` could ride a CAS payload
   558	        // that decodes as `Verify` — sequencer would have written that
   559	        // mismatch but replay would have silently accepted it pre-v1.1.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   560	        let decoded_kind = typed_tx.tx_kind();
   561	        if decoded_kind != entry.tx_kind {
   562	            return Err(ReplayError::TxKindMismatch {
   563	                at: i,
   564	                envelope_kind: entry.tx_kind,
   565	                decoded_kind,
   566	            });
   567	        }
   568	
   569	        // Stage 7: re-run pure dispatch_transition.

   592	
   593	/// Skeleton-stage entry point (v1.1).
   594	///
   595	/// Validates:
   596	/// 1. logical_t monotonicity (no gaps, no duplicates)
   597	/// 2. parent_state_root chain
   598	/// 3. parent_ledger_root chain (K2 transplant defense)
   599	/// 4. resulting_ledger_root recomputed via append(prev_ledger_root, signing_digest)
   600	///
   601	/// Does NOT verify:
   602	/// - system_signature (CO1.7.5+: requires CanonicalMessage extension wired through keypair)
   603	/// - resulting_state_root (CO1.7.5+: requires dispatch_transition + CO1.4-extra CAS persistence)
   604	///
   605	/// Returns final (state_root, ledger_root) on success.
   606	pub fn replay_chain_integrity(
   607	    genesis_state_root: Hash,
   608	    genesis_ledger_root: Hash,
   609	    entries: &[LedgerEntry],
   610	) -> Result<(Hash, Hash), ReplayError> {
   611	    let mut prev_state_root = genesis_state_root;
   612	    let mut prev_ledger_root = genesis_ledger_root;
   613	
   614	    for (i, entry) in entries.iter().enumerate() {
   615	        let expected_logical_t = (i as u64) + 1;
   616	        if entry.logical_t != expected_logical_t {
   617	            return Err(ReplayError::LogicalTGap {
   618	                at: i,
   619	                expected: expected_logical_t,
   620	                got: entry.logical_t,
   621	            });
   622	        }
   623	        if entry.parent_state_root != prev_state_root {
   624	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentStateMismatch { at: i });
   625	        }
   626	        // K2 NEW: parent_ledger_root chain check
   627	        if entry.parent_ledger_root != prev_ledger_root {
   628	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentLedgerMismatch { at: i });
   629	        }
   630	        let signing_digest = entry.to_signing_payload().canonical_digest();
   631	        let recomputed = append(&prev_ledger_root, &signing_digest);
   632	        if recomputed != entry.resulting_ledger_root {
   633	            return Err(ReplayError::LedgerRootMismatch { at: i });
   634	        }
   635	        prev_state_root = entry.resulting_state_root;
   636	        prev_ledger_root = entry.resulting_ledger_root;
   637	    }
   638	
   639	    Ok((prev_state_root, prev_ledger_root))
   640	}
   872	pub(crate) fn dispatch_transition(
   873	    q: &QState,
   874	    tx: &TypedTx,
   875	    _predicate_registry: &PredicateRegistry,
   876	    _tool_registry: &ToolRegistry,
   877	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   878	    match tx {
   879	        TypedTx::Work(work) => {
   880	            // TB-2 Atom 3: WorkTx pure validation per preflight v3 §3.3.
   881	            // No I/O, no side effects, no writer calls — apply_one is the
   882	            // only place ledger writes happen.
   883	
   884	            // Step 1: parent-root match (Inv 5; P1:5).
   885	            if work.parent_state_root != q.state_root_t {
   886	                return Err(TransitionError::StaleParent);
   887	            }
   888	
   889	            // Step 2: acceptance predicate bundle — every entry must be true.
   890	            for (pid, bwp) in work.predicate_results.acceptance.iter() {
   891	                if !bwp.value {
   892	                    return Err(TransitionError::AcceptancePredicateFailed(pid.clone()));
   893	                }
   894	            }
   895	
   896	            // Step 3: settlement predicate bundle (if applicable to RSP-1).
   897	            for (pid, bwp) in work.predicate_results.settlement.iter() {
   898	                if !bwp.value {
   899	                    return Err(TransitionError::SettlementPredicateFailed(pid.clone()));
   900	                }
   901	            }
   902	
   903	            // Step 4: YES stake gate (RSP-1 P3:3). StakeMicroCoin newtype
   904	            // intentionally has no integer comparison; use the const accessor.
   905	            //
   906	            // TB-N1-AGENT-ECONOMY Phase 2 A3 (2026-05-10) extends Step 4 with
   907	            // an agent-bound upper-side gate: agent must not declare a stake
   908	            // that exceeds their `balances_t` entry. Distinct from
   909	            // `InsufficientBalance` at Step 6 (system-side debit-time
   910	            // solvency defense-in-depth — same inequality, different
   911	            // semantic surface). Reading the balance here matches the
   912	            // Step 6 read pattern (default-zero on missing entry per
   913	            // existing fail-closed admission discipline).
   914	            if work.stake.micro_units() <= 0 {
   915	                return Err(TransitionError::StakeInsufficient);
   916	            }
   917	            let agent_balance_a3 = q.economic_state_t.balances_t.0
   918	                .get(&work.agent_id)
   919	                .copied()
   920	                .unwrap_or(crate::economy::money::MicroCoin::zero());
   921	            if work.stake.micro_units() > agent_balance_a3.micro_units() {
   922	                return Err(TransitionError::StakeBalanceExceeded);
   923	            }
   924	
   925	            // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   926	            // TB-3 Atom 6 — Bridge DELETED. Structural admission via the
   927	            // formal RSP-1 surface: task_markets_t[task_id].total_escrow > 0.
   928	            // The TB-2 P0-B option (a) bridge `TxId(work.task_id.0.clone())`
   929	            // synthetic-ID + escrows_t fallback is GONE — its constitutional
   930	            // debt is now closed. Charter § 4.3 step 6 + § 5 #14 (no bridge
   931	            // resurrection — enforced by tests/tb_3_bridge_deletion_invariant.rs
   932	            // in Atom 7).
   933	            // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   934	
   935	            // Step 5: escrow presence gate via formal surface (charter § 4.3
   936	            // step 6 NEW form). task_markets_t is now TaskId-keyed and
   937	            // populated only by accepted TaskOpenTx. total_escrow is the
   938	            // derived cache that grows only via accepted EscrowLockTx.
   939	            let market = q.economic_state_t.task_markets_t.0.get(&work.task_id);
   940	            let has_escrow = market.map_or(false, |m| m.total_escrow.micro_units() > 0);
   941	            if !has_escrow {
   942	                return Err(TransitionError::EscrowMissing);
   943	            }
   944	
   945	            // Step 6: solver solvency gate (charter § 4.3 step 7 NEW). Per
   946	            // WP § 14.1 + § 18 Inv 5, accepted WorkTx commits stake by
   947	            // debiting balance — solver must hold ≥ work.stake.coin.
   948	            let solver_bal = q.economic_state_t.balances_t.0
   949	                .get(&work.agent_id)
   950	                .copied()
   951	                .unwrap_or(crate::economy::money::MicroCoin::zero());
   952	            if solver_bal.micro_units() < work.stake.micro_units() {
   953	                return Err(TransitionError::InsufficientBalance);
   954	            }
   955	
   956	            // Step 7: monetary invariants ordering (existing TB-2; same shape).
   957	            assert_no_post_init_mint(tx, q)
   958	                .map_err(|_| TransitionError::MonetaryInvariantViolation)?;
   959	            assert_read_is_free(tx.tx_kind(), 0)
   960	                .map_err(|_| TransitionError::MonetaryInvariantViolation)?;
   961	
   962	            // Step 8: build q_next. **TB-3 NEW (charter § 3.4 lock-on-accept)**:
   963	            // accepted WorkTx atomically debits balance + locks stake into
   964	            // stakes_t. Per WP § 18 Inv 5 the YES stake is event-bound to
   965	            // the WorkTx itself; per Law 2 ("Only Investment Costs Money")
   966	            // investment is consumed at commitment. CTF is conserved
   967	            // (debit balance = credit stakes); no mint, no burn.
   968	            let mut q_next = q.clone();
   969	            let new_bal_micro = solver_bal.micro_units() - work.stake.micro_units();
   970	            q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.0.insert(
   971	                work.agent_id.clone(),
   972	                crate::economy::money::MicroCoin::from_micro_units(new_bal_micro),
   973	            );
   974	            q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.0.insert(
   975	                work.tx_id.clone(),
   976	                crate::state::q_state::StakeEntry {
   977	                    // StakeMicroCoin(pub MicroCoin) — unwrap the inner
   978	                    // MicroCoin (StakesIndex.amount: MicroCoin per q_state.rs).
   979	                    amount: work.stake.0,
   980	                    staker: work.agent_id.clone(),
   981	                    task_id: work.task_id.clone(),
   982	                },
   983	            );
   984	            // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   985	            // TB-12 Atom 2 (architect 2026-05-03 ruling §3 + §8 Atom 2):
   986	            // accepted WorkTx with stake > 0 derives a `FirstLong`
   987	            // NodePosition exposure record. Pure additive index write —
   988	            // **no money mutation**, **no change** to balances_t / stakes_t
   989	            // / total_supply (those are handled above by TB-3 economic
   990	            // logic). NodePosition.amount is **NOT a Coin holding** per
   991	            // CR-12.1 + CR-12.2; the 5-holding CTF sum stays unchanged.
   992	            // FR-12.1 + FR-12.4: kind = FirstLong; node_id = work.tx_id;
   993	            // position_id = source_tx = work.tx_id (one-source-tx-one-position
   994	            // invariant for TB-12 per architect §4 last paragraph).
   995	            // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   996	            if work.stake.micro_units() > 0 {
   997	                let position = crate::state::typed_tx::NodePosition {
   998	                    position_id: work.tx_id.clone(),
   999	                    node_id: work.tx_id.clone(),
  1000	                    task_id: work.task_id.clone(),
  1001	                    owner: work.agent_id.clone(),
  1002	                    side: crate::state::typed_tx::PositionSide::Long,
  1003	                    kind: crate::state::typed_tx::PositionKind::FirstLong,
  1004	                    amount: work.stake.0,
  1005	                    source_tx: work.tx_id.clone(),
  1006	                    opened_at_round: work.timestamp_logical,
  1007	                };
  1008	                q_next
  1009	                    .economic_state_t
  1010	                    .node_positions_t
  1011	                    .0
  1012	                    .insert(work.tx_id.clone(), position);
  1013	            }
  1014	            // state_root advance (existing TB-2; WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1).
  1015	            q_next.state_root_t = worktx_accept_state_root(&q.state_root_t, tx);
  1016	
  1017	            // Step 9: conservation now does REAL work — not a no-op as in
  1018	            // TB-2. The debit-to-stakes invariant is the primary CTF check
  1019	            // on the runtime spine. Production runtime ALWAYS passes `&[]`
  1020	            // (charter § 5 red line 3 / TB-2 #4 inherited).
  1021	            assert_total_ctf_conserved(
  1022	                &q.economic_state_t,
  1023	                &q_next.economic_state_t,
  1024	                &[],
  1025	            )

   820	//
   821	// In-crate unit tests cover the construction path that does NOT need the
   822	// full evaluator / TuringBus surface. Integration tests for the full L4 path
   823	// (including direct `bus.submit_typed_tx` fixture) live at
   824	// `tests/tb_6_runtime_chaintape_bootstrap.rs` and land in Atom 1.3.
   825	
   826	#[cfg(test)]
   827	mod tests {
   828	    use super::*;
   829	    use tempfile::TempDir;
   830	
   831	    fn cfg_for(tmp: &TempDir, run_id: &str) -> RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
   832	        RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
   833	            runtime_repo_path: tmp.path().join("runtime_repo"),
   834	            cas_path: tmp.path().join("cas"),
   835	            run_id: run_id.to_string(),
   836	            queue_capacity: 16,
   837	            resume_existing_chain: false,
   838	        }
   839	    }
   840	
   841	    #[tokio::test]
   842	    async fn build_chaintape_sequencer_returns_non_none_sequencer_with_git_writer() {
   843	        let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   844	        let cfg = cfg_for(&tmp, "t1-run");
   845	        let bundle = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg).expect("bootstrap");
   846	        // Sequencer constructed.
   847	        assert_eq!(Arc::strong_count(&bundle.sequencer) >= 2, true);
   848	        // pinned_pubkeys.json was written.
   849	        let manifest_path = cfg.runtime_repo_path.join(PINNED_PUBKEYS_FILENAME);
   850	        assert!(manifest_path.exists(), "pinned_pubkeys.json must exist at {manifest_path:?}");
   851	        // Clean shutdown.
   852	        bundle.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown");
   853	    }
   854	
   855	    #[tokio::test]
   856	    async fn build_chaintape_sequencer_writes_pinned_pubkeys_json_to_runtime_repo() {
   857	        let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   858	        let cfg = cfg_for(&tmp, "t2-run");
   859	        let bundle = build_chaintape_sequencer(&cfg).expect("bootstrap");
   860	        let manifest_path = cfg.runtime_repo_path.join(PINNED_PUBKEYS_FILENAME);
   861	        let json = std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path).expect("read manifest");
   862	        let manifest: PinnedPubkeyManifest =
   863	            serde_json::from_str(&json).expect("parse manifest");
   864	        assert_eq!(manifest.run_id, "t2-run");
   865	        assert_eq!(manifest.tb_id, "TB-6");
   866	        assert_eq!(manifest.epoch, 1);
   867	        assert_eq!(manifest.pubkeys.len(), 1);
   868	        assert!(!manifest.pubkeys[0].pubkey_hex.is_empty());
   869	        bundle.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown");
   870	    }
   871	
   872	    #[tokio::test]
   873	    async fn build_chaintape_sequencer_fails_on_non_empty_repo() {
   874	        let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   875	        let cfg = cfg_for(&tmp, "t3-run");
   876	        // First bootstrap on empty repo — succeeds.

codex
Q1: PASS — Manifest/init-Q/read/replay errors return `BootstrapError` before sequencer construction.
Q2: PASS — Resume manifest read is deterministic; only new keypair generation is random as expected.
Q3: PASS — `Sequencer::new` delegates directly to `new_at_logical_t(.., 0)`.
Q4: PASS — Env gate is strict `Ok("1")`, no truthy parsing.
Q5: PASS — SG-G1.4 exists, `constitution_g1_resume` is registered, and 5/5 targeted tests pass.
Q6: PASS — Trust Root hashes match current `sha256sum`.
Q7: PASS — Resume surface adds no `f64`, shadow ledger, or global Markov pointer.
Q8: PASS — Default `resume_existing_chain: false` preserves `NonEmptyRuntimeRepo`.
Q9: PASS — `TRACE_FLOWCHART_MATRIX` has explicit FC2-INV8 resume row.
Q10: PASS — L4 replays via `replay_full_transition`; valid L4.E is verified as evidence-only.
Q11: PASS — Old pubkeys are preserved and new epoch key is appended/pinned.
Q12: VETO — No repo/manifest single-writer lock or CAS update; concurrent bundles can race epoch append and L4 ref commits.

Aggregate verdict: VETO
Conviction: high
Recommendation: HALT

codex
## Constitutional Alignment Note
Verdict: PASS. Conviction: high.  
The deviation is correct: `HeadTWitness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` requires caller-supplied `state_root` and `economic_state_root`, so it is a derived-view helper, not a QState replay primitive. The resume path correctly uses `replay_full_transition`, matching `verify_chaintape`.

## Q1
Verdict: PASS. Conviction: high.  
`bootstrap_resume_state` fail-closes on missing/parse-failed `pinned_pubkeys.json`, missing/parse-failed `initial_q_state.json`, `read_at` sweep errors, and `replay_full_transition` errors before constructing the resumed sequencer.

## Q2
Verdict: PASS. Conviction: high.  
The existing manifest read is deterministic: filesystem read, serde parse, ordered iteration into `PinnedSystemPubkeys`. The only randomness is after replay, for the new epoch keypair, which is expected.

## Q3
Verdict: PASS. Conviction: high.  
`Sequencer::new` is a thin delegation to `Sequencer::new_at_logical_t(..., 0)`, and the companion constructor only changes the `next_logical_t` seed. Admission arms and transition logic remain shared.

## Q4
Verdict: PASS. Conviction: high.  
The env gate is strict equality: `matches!(std::env::var("TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME").as_deref(), Ok("1"))`. No truthy-string widening.

## Q5
Verdict: PASS. Conviction: high.  
SG-G1.4 is present in `tests/constitution_g1_resume.rs` and asserts `NonEmptyRuntimeRepo` still fires under `resume_existing_chain: false`. `constitution_g1_resume` is registered in `scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh`, whose loop runs each gate via `cargo test --test`.

## Q6

Trust Root entries match current file bytes.
